speaker: Unknown
en: Used to have about 10 people full-time.
zh: 以前有大约 10 名全职员工。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you have 1.2 humans, 20 agents.
zh: 现在你有 1.2 个人类，20 个智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have 10 desks that used to be go-to market people.
zh: 我们有 10 张办公桌，以前是给市场人员的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all just labeled with our agents, Repli for Replit, Quali for Qualified, Arti for Artisan.
zh: 它们现在都贴着我们智能体的标签，Repli 代表 Replit，Quali 代表 Qualified，Arti 代表 Artisan。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agent Force needs a nickname.
zh: Agent Force 需要一个昵称。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agents work all night and they work weekends and they work on Christmas.
zh: 智能体整晚工作，周末也工作，圣诞节也工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're done with hiring humans in sales.
zh: 我们不再招聘人类做销售了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're done.
zh: 完毕。

speaker: Unknown
en: The business is doing very similarly to what it was when you had 10 humans.
zh: 业务表现和有 10 个人的时候非常相似。

speaker: Unknown
en: If I had two more great humans that wanted to join, don't get me wrong, I would hire them tomorrow, but I'm not going to hire someone that after their third month in the job doesn't know what SaaStr does.
zh: 如果我再有两个想加入的优秀人类，别误会，我明天就会雇佣他们，但我不会雇佣那些在工作三个月后还不知道 SaaStr 是做什么的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just can't do that.
zh: 我就是不能那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is replacing the jobs people don't want to do today, and it is displacing the midpack and the mediocre.
zh: AI 正在取代人们今天不想做的工作，并且它正在挤出中游和平庸的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you see the future of the sales profession?
zh: 你如何看待销售职业的未来？

speaker: Unknown
en: We should have $250,000 a year SDRs, but they'd be like ... At Vercel, they'd be managing 10 agents, not 10 people.
zh: 我们应该有年薪 25 万美元的 SDR，但他们应该像……在 Vercel，他们会管理 10 个智能体，而不是 10 个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: The classic SDR junior kid that is hired out of college to send emails, we don't need them.
zh: 那种大学毕业后被雇佣来发邮件的经典初级 SDR 小孩，我们不需要他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: Folks that qualify leads coming in, the contact mes that we see, we have no need for them today.
zh: 那些审核进来的线索、我们看到的联系人，我们今天不再需要他们了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They should be extinct next year.
zh: 他们明年应该灭绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone's listening to us like, "Oh man, my job is in trouble."
zh: 有人听我们说话时会想，“天哪，我的工作有麻烦了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If you can go do this, you're hyper-employable.
zh: 如果你能去做这个，你就极具就业能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, my guest is Jason Lemkin, founder and CEO of SaaStr, the world's largest community for B2B founders, and one of my absolute favorite sales and go-to market minds on the planet.
zh: 今天我的嘉宾是 Jason Lemkin，SaaStr 的创始人兼 CEO，这是世界上为 B2B 创始人打造的最大社区，也是我在这个星球上绝对最喜欢的销售和市场思维之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jason is not only deeply knowledgeable about everything sales, he's also extremely articulate and direct, and is also now personally going super deep on what AI can do for a sales org.
zh: Jason 不仅对所有销售知识渊博，他也非常善于表达和直接，而且现在他还亲自深入研究 AI 能为销售组织做什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's transformed his own SaaStr sales team from around 10 SDRs and AEs to one full-time AE, a part-time chief of AI, named Amelia, and 20 AI agents.
zh: 他已经将他自己的 SaaStr 销售团队从大约 10 名 SDR 和 AE 转变为一名全职 AE，一名兼职 AI 负责人，名叫 Amelia，以及 20 个 AI 智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: He is seeing the same performance from his AI team as he saw with his former human team, and he's just getting started.
zh: 他从他的 AI 团队看到的业绩和他以前的人类团队看到的业绩一样，而他才刚刚开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are my favorite kinds of conversations because the guest is living in the future and comes here to show us what the future is like, where we're headed, and how we can get there ourselves, and also just how to avoid all the pitfalls that he had to deal with along the way.
zh: 这些是我最喜欢的一类对话，因为嘉宾生活在未来，来到这里向我们展示未来是什么样的，我们要去向何方，以及我们自己如何到达那里，还有就是要避免他一路上不得不处理的所有陷阱。

speaker: Unknown
en: We cover all of the things that he has learned about where sales and go-to market is going in the AI age.
zh: 我们涵盖了他了解到的关于销售和市场在 AI 时代走向的所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: He gives a bunch of advice for salespeople and the future of their careers, the future of the go-to market org, how to win as an AI startup right now, what tools he's finding most useful, what it took to shift a sales team, and so much more.
zh: 他为销售人员和他们职业生涯的未来、市场组织的未来、作为 AI 初创公司如何获胜、他发现哪些工具最有用、转换销售团队需要什么提供了大量建议，以及更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is going to get your mind spinning in the best way possible.
zh: 这一集将以最好的方式让你的大脑飞速旋转。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you enjoy this podcast, don't forget to subscribe and follow it in your favorite podcasting app or YouTube.
zh: 如果你喜欢这个播客，别忘了在你最喜欢的播客应用或 YouTube 上订阅和关注。

speaker: Unknown
en: It helps tremendously.
zh: 这有很大帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you become an annual subscriber of my newsletter, you get 19 premium products for free for an entire year.
zh: 如果你成为我简讯的年度订阅者，你将在一整年内免费获得 19 种高级产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Including a year free of Lovable, Replit, Bolt, N8N, Gamma, Linear, Devin, Posthog, Superhuman, Descript, Wspr Flow, Perplexity, Warp, Granola, Magic Patterns, Raycast, ChatPRD, Mobbin, and Stripe Atlas.
zh: 包括免费使用一年的 Lovable、Replit、Bolt、N8N、Gamma、Linear、Devin、Posthog、Superhuman、Descript、Wspr Flow、Perplexity、Warp、Granola、Magic Patterns、Raycast、ChatPRD、Mobbin 和 Stripe Atlas。

speaker: Unknown
en: Head on over to lennysnewsletter.com and click product pass.
zh: 前往 lennysnewsletter.com 并点击 product pass。

speaker: Unknown
en: With that, I'd bring you Jason Lemkin after a short word from our sponsors.
zh: 话虽如此，在简短的赞助商广告之后，我会带来 Jason Lemkin。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today's episode is brought to you by DX, the developer intelligence platform designed by leading researchers.
zh: 今天的节目由 DX 带给你，DX 是由领先研究人员设计的开发者智能平台。

speaker: Unknown
en: To thrive in the AI era, organizations need to adapt quickly, but many organization leaders struggle to answer pressing questions like, which tools are working?
zh: 要在 AI 时代蓬勃发展，组织需要快速适应，但许多组织领导者难以回答紧迫的问题，比如哪些工具在起作用？

speaker: Unknown
en: How are they being used?
zh: 它们是如何被使用的？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's actually driving value?
zh: 实际上是什么在推动价值？

speaker: Unknown
en: DX provides the data and insights that leaders need to navigate this shift.
zh: DX 提供领导者导航这种转变所需的数据和洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: With DX, companies like Dropbox, Booking.com, Adyen, and Intercom get a deep understanding of how AI is providing value to their developers and what impact AI is having on engineering productivity.
zh: 借助 DX，像 Dropbox、Booking.com、Adyen 和 Intercom 这样的公司能深入了解 AI 如何为他们的开发者提供价值，以及 AI 对工程生产力有什么影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: To learn more, visit DX's website at getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 要了解更多，请访问 DX 的网站 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's getdx.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 getdx.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by v0 from Vercel.
zh: 这一集由 Vercel 的 v0 带给你。

speaker: Unknown
en: v0 is the web development assistant designed for professionals of all technical backgrounds.
zh: v0 是为所有技术背景的专业人士设计的网络开发助手。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether you're a product manager, designer, or developer.
zh: 无论你是产品经理、设计师还是开发者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Transform how you bring products to life.
zh: 转变你将产品变为现实的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: With v0, everybody can cook.
zh: 有了 v0，每个人都能做饭。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't just show up to reviews with docs and ideas.
zh: 不要只带着文档和想法来参加评审。

speaker: Unknown
en: Arrive with working prototypes that demonstrate real functionality.
zh: 带着能展示真实功能的可用原型来。

speaker: Unknown
en: v0 drafts project plans, generates interactive interfaces, and builds full stack applications without writing a single line of code.
zh: v0 起草项目计划，生成交互式界面，并构建全栈应用程序，而无需编写一行代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: And with features like AI and database integrations, screenshot import, and sync with GitHub, v0 helps reduce development bottlenecks and enhance collaboration between technical and non-technical team members.
zh: 并且具有 AI 和数据库集成、截图导入以及与 GitHub 同步等功能，v0 帮助减少开发瓶颈，并增强技术和非技术团队成员之间的协作。

speaker: Unknown
en: The result, faster iteration, and a shorter path from idea to implementation.
zh: 结果就是更快的迭代，以及从想法到实现的更短路径。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vercel built v0 for the builders who want to create at the moment of inspiration.
zh: Vercel 构建 v0 是为了那些想在灵感迸发时刻进行创造的建设者。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you can dream it, you can ship it.
zh: 只要你能梦见它，你就能发布它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit vercel.com/lenny to get started.
zh: 访问 vercel.com/lenny 以开始使用。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's vercel.com/lenny.
zh: 那就是 vercel.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Jason, thank you so much for being here and welcome back to the podcast.
zh: Jason，非常感谢你能来到这里，欢迎回到播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lucky to be a super fan and then, to get to join, right?
zh: 很幸运能成为超级粉丝，然后能加入进来，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's terrific to be on the other side.
zh: 坐在这个位置感觉棒极了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is our second conversation.
zh: 所以这是我们的第二次对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: We did our last conversation a year and a half ago.
zh: 我们的上一次对话是在一年半以前。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you know this, but it became a pretty legendary episode.
zh: 我不知道你是否知道这一点，但它成为了一集相当传奇的节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's something people continue to share and that conversation is around basically a deep dive into building your sales org.
zh: 这是人们持续分享的一期，那场对话基本上是关于深入构建销售组织的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot has changed for you and the world since then.
zh: 从那时起，你和世界都发生了很大变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Kauf AI, and what's happened is you've gone extremely deep on what AI enables for sales, for startups, for go-to market.
zh: Kauf AI，发生的事情是，你已经对 AI 能为销售、初创公司和 go-to market 带来的什么极其深入了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I love about conversations like this is you're basically living in the future.
zh: 我喜欢这样的对话的原因是，你基本上生活在未来。 [[65 ]] 你在这里给我们一个事物走向的概览，告诉我们如何避开错误的道路，并帮助我们自己去到那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you're here to give us a glimpse of where things are heading and tell us how to avoid the wrong turns and just help us get there ourselves.
zh: 而你在这里是为了让我们一窥事物的发展方向，并告诉我们如何避开弯路，只是帮助我们到达那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: That I think I can do.
zh: 我想我能做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: To start.
zh: 开始吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Help us understand just the business you run, this SaaStr.
zh: 帮我们了解一下你经营的业务，这个 SaaStr。

speaker: Jason Lemkin
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is it you sell?
zh: 你卖的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What is the business?
zh: 这个业务是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What is it you do, Jason?
zh: 你是做什么的，Jason？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm still trying to figure it out, Lenny.
zh: 我还在试图弄清楚这一点，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe you are too in some ways.
zh: 也许你在某些方面也是。

speaker: Lenny
en: Yeah, that's true.
zh: 是的，那是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I am a two-time founder who started writing a blog, my God, in 2012 about all the mistakes I made after I sold my startup to Adobe.
zh: 所以我是一个两度创业的创始人，在 2012 年，天哪，开始写一篇博客，是关于我把初创公司卖给 Adobe 之后犯的所有错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: We started doing a couple meetups.
zh: 我们开始举办一些聚会。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've done meetups.
zh: 你做过聚会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Before anyone did this stuff, we did a big meetup in 2015, thousands of people came.
zh: 在任何人做这些事情之前，我们在 2015 年举办了一个大型聚会，成千上万的人来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then we do 10,000 people a year at SaaStr Annual.
zh: 然后我们在 SaaStr Annual 每年接待 10,000 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've actually also invested almost 200 million dollars, almost 10X lifetime, only into founders from the community.
zh: 事实上，我也投资了近 2 亿美元，近 10 倍的终身回报，只投资给来自社区的创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the reason I don't know is what I ... Folks that are connected to our content, I'm just passionate about helping other founders see mistakes and make less of them.
zh: 但我不知道的原因是……与我们的内容相关的人，我只是热衷于帮助其他创始人看到错误并少犯错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyhow, we're a large community.
zh: 无论如何，我们是一个大型社区。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do invest, but it turned into a business.
zh: 我确实做投资，但它变成了一项业务。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was no revenue in the beginning.
zh: 开始时没有收入。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure there was no revenue, Lenny in the beginning.
zh: 我确定开始时没有收入，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: And ours was less intentional, but we do do eight figures of revenue a year and it's work.
zh: 我们的意图性没那么强，但我们每年确实有八位数的收入，而且这得干活。

speaker: Unknown
en: That sounds great to do eight figures, but there's a lot of costs, especially on the event side.
zh: 做到八位数听起来很棒，但有很多成本，特别是在活动方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: The media side has almost no cost.
zh: 媒体方面几乎没有成本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's work.
zh: 而且得干活。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have a hundred sponsors.
zh: 我们有一百个赞助商。 [[93 ] 正如你所知……你有赞助商，但在一定层面上它变成了很多工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as you know ... You have sponsors, but there's a certain level where it becomes a lot of work.
zh: 如你所知……你有赞助商，但到了某个程度，这会变成大量的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Getting two folks to sponsor your podcast with a couple emails, no work.
zh: 发几封邮件让两个人赞助你的播客，不费事。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you wanted to have four Lenny podcast, it just scales.
zh: 如果你想拥有四个 Lenny 播客，它就能扩展。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have built our own go-to market team over time, and I've lived the frustrations.
zh: 所以我们随着时间的推移建立我们自己的 go-to market 团队，我也经历过那些挫败。

speaker: Unknown
en: Folks have followed it.
zh: 人们一直关注着它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, maybe I'm rambling a little bit.
zh: 然后，也许我有点啰嗦了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The interesting thing, the aha moment that happened to us is going into May of this year, we had one AI agent in production called Delphi that we both use for digital lending and Digital Jason.
zh: 有趣的是，我们发生的顿悟时刻是进入今年五月，我们在生产环境中有一个 AI 智能体叫 Delphi，我们俩都把它用于数字借贷和 Digital Jason。

speaker: Unknown
en: Really interesting learnings.
zh: 真的很有趣的收获。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we went into our 10,000 person event this year with a seven-figure budget and eight-figure top line.
zh: 今年我们带着 7 位数的预算和 8 位数的顶级收入进入了我们的一万人活动。

speaker: Unknown
en: And two folks on the sales team who were paid high end of market.
zh: 还有两名销售团队的人，他们是按市场高端薪酬支付的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have many flaws, but paying well and being loyal are not one of them.
zh: 我有很多缺点，但付得好和忠诚不是其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And two of them just quit at the event.
zh: 而且他们中的两个人就在活动上辞职了。

speaker: Unknown
en: They just quit on site.
zh: 他们就在现场辞职了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is like the third time I've done this, like the eighth team I've built, and I turned to Amelia, our chief AI officer, and I said, "We're done with hiring humans in sales.
zh: 这就像我第三次做这种事了，就像我建立的第八个团队，我转向我们的首席 AI 官 Amelia，我说，“我们在销售领域雇佣人类这件事已经结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're done.
zh: 我们结束了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We are going to push the limits with agents.
zh: 我们将用智能体来突破极限。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to put..."
zh: 我们将……”

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if it doesn't quite work.
zh: 即使它并不完全奏效。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I knew from this Delphi, this general agent that it would sort of work because going into annual, this general agent, this Digital Jason closed a 70K sponsorship on its own.
zh: 我从这个 Delphi，这个通用智能体知道它会奏效一点，因为在进入年度之前，这个通用智能体，这个数字 Jason 独自完成了一个 7 万美元的赞助。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when I saw that a horizontal agent, not trained for sales, not trained for GTM could close one deal, they're like, "Let's deploy a couple of these apps.
zh: 所以当我看到一个横向智能体，不是为销售训练的，不是为 GTM 训练的，能完成一笔交易，他们就像，“让我们部署几个这样的应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have time.
zh: 我们有时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just can't pay a junior SDR $150,000 a year to quit.
zh: 我真的无法每年付给一个初级 SDR 15 万美元结果他们却辞职。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just can't ... criticize me, but I just couldn't do it one more time.
zh: 我真的无法……批评我吧，但我真的不能再做一次了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just couldn't do it one more."
zh: 我真的不能再做一次了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I actually think this isn't ... When I talk to CEOs at leading AI companies, they kind of don't want to do it either.
zh: 我其实认为这不是……当我与领先 AI 公司的 CEO 交谈时，他们其实也不想这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want to have the smallest sales teams they can as much for cultural reasons.
zh: 他们想要拥有尽可能最小的销售团队，很大程度上是出于文化原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if Replit only goes from zero to 200, it could have been 220 with the smaller sales team.
zh: 即使 Replit 只是从 0 增长到 200，它本可以用更小的销售团队达到 220。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think I'm just okay with it.
zh: 我觉得我只是对此没问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's an enduring theme.
zh: 所以这是一个持久的主题。

speaker: Unknown
en: But anyhow, so we pushed the limits.
zh: 但不管怎样，所以我们突破了极限。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now if you walk into SaaStr's office, it's kind of funny.
zh: 现在如果你走进 SaaStr 的办公室，这有点好笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have 10 desks that used to be go-to market people.
zh: 我们有 10 个以前是 go-to market 人员的桌子。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all just labeled with our agents, Repli for Replit, Quali for Qualified, Arti for Artisan.
zh: 它们现在都贴着我们智能体的标签，Repli 代表 Replit，Quali 代表 Qualified，Arti 代表 Artisan。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agentforce needs a nickname.
zh: Agentforce 需要一个绰号。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe we can make one up with Salesforce.
zh: 也许我们可以和 Salesforce 一起编一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's Amelia's corner office at one end.
zh: 在一端有 Amelia 的角落办公室。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm in the back of the office and it's just agents.
zh: 我在办公室的后面，那里只有智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the quietest office.

speaker: Unknown
en: Now, everyone, not the traditional ... The traditional metric was in most categories, three to 5% of prospects would be in market a year.
zh: 现在，所有人，而不是传统的……传统指标是在大多数类别中，每年有 3% 到 5% 的潜在客户会在市场上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you'd send a trillion emails and you'd do cold calls and you'd hope maybe 2026 was the time they were willing to dump Salesforce for your new product.
zh: 所以你会发送一万亿封电子邮件，你会进行陌生电话拜访，你希望也许 2026 年是他们愿意抛弃 Salesforce 转而使用你的新产品的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: So add all that up, 5%.
zh: 所以把所有这些加起来，5%。

speaker: Unknown
en: And many categories work north of 50% in market.
zh: 而许多类别中有超过 50% 的人在市场上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that just totally changes ... the plays still work.
zh: 所以这彻底改变了……那些策略仍然有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: Showing up in person, actually knowing what the hell you're selling, knowing how to get through procurement, all of those that work.
zh: 亲自出现，真正知道你在卖什么鬼东西，知道如何通过采购流程，所有这些都有效。

speaker: Unknown
en: But other than these weird windows, artificial windows in 2020, we've never had so many people in market at once.
zh: 但除了这些奇怪的窗口期，2020 年的人工窗口期之外，我们从未有过这么多人同时在这个市场上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is for AI products specifically or beyond that?
zh: 这专门针对 AI 产品还是超越于此？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, that have massive ROI.
zh: 是的，那些有巨大 ROI 的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Productivity.
zh: 生产力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to bring a Vibecode tool into my company, Lenny.
zh: 我需要把一个 Vibecode 工具带进我的公司，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, go out and do the work, compare Replit, Lovable, and whoever else and buy one.
zh: 好的，出去做工作，比较 Replit、Lovable 和其他任何人，然后买一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why are Harvey and the others and the Goro are doing ... I mean, they're great tools, but everyone is like, we need to automate how we review contracts and documents with AI now.
zh: 为什么 Harvey 和其他人以及 Goro 做得……我是说，它们是很棒的工具，但每个人都说，我们现在需要用 AI 自动化我们审查合同和文档的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want a leader and they're going to do it.
zh: 他们想要一个领导者，并且他们会去做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that will slow down.
zh: 而这会慢下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not everybody can be in market every year.
zh: 不是每个人都能每年都在市场上。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's an exhausting enterprise.
zh: 这是一项令人精疲力竭的事业。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is a version of the AI bubble that will end and we will revert in some ways to old school, but when everybody is in market, it just changes how you run the whole thing.
zh: 所以这是 AI 泡沫的一个版本，它将会结束，我们在某些方面会回归旧的方式，但当每个人都在市场上时，这就会改变你运作整个事情的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the fastest growing ones and the slowest growing ones both have incentives to use AI here, just for different reasons.
zh: 所以增长最快的那些和增长最慢的那些都有动力在这里使用 AI，只是原因不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: How about the sales profession specifically?
zh: 具体到销售职业怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are SDRs going to be replaced fully AEs?
zh: SDR 会被完全取代吗，AE 呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you see the future of the sales profession?
zh: 你如何看待销售职业的未来？

speaker: Unknown
en: The classic SDR junior kid that is hired out of college to send emails and respond to inbound emails and maybe get back to them later that day or the next day, we don't need them.
zh: 那个典型的 SDR，那个刚大学毕业被雇佣来发邮件并回复入站邮件，也许在同一天晚些时候或第二天回复他们的孩子，我们不需要他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not going to need most of them.
zh: 我们将不再需要他们中的大多数人。

speaker: Unknown
en: SDRs that knock on doors in a lot of industries aren't going to be displaced.
zh: 在很多行业中敲门的 SDR 不会被取代。

speaker: Unknown
en: The email-based cadence SDR will be 90% displaced by AI next year.
zh: 基于邮件节奏的 SDR 明年将被 AI 取代 90%。

speaker: Unknown
en: People have different nomenclature.
zh: 人们有不同的命名法。

speaker: Unknown
en: I call BDRs, folks that qualify leads coming in, the contact needs that we see.
zh: 我称 BDR 为那些对进来的线索进行资格认定的人，我们看到的联系需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have no need for them today.
zh: 我们今天对它们没有需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: They should be extinct next year.
zh: 它们明年应该灭绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is no reason in the age of AI.
zh: 在 AI 时代没有理由这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have Hit contact me, wait a day or two for a 21-year-old that doesn't know what Linear does to say, "Hey, what do you do?
zh: 让 Hit 联系我，等一两天，让一个不知道 Linear 做什么的 21 岁的人说，“嘿，你是做什么的？

speaker: Unknown
en: How much are you willing to pay me?
zh: 你愿意付给我多少钱？

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I'll set up a call with Lenny later this week."
zh: 也许我这周晚些时候会和 Lenny 安排一个电话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: There is no need to do that with AI.
zh: 用 AI 没有必要那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Our AI alone, one of our agent fully qualifies everybody on the website, so they don't even know they're being qualified and just sets up the meeting with the salesperson.
zh: 光是我们的 AI，我们的一个智能体就能完全对网站上的每个人进行资格认定，所以他们甚至不知道自己正在被资格认定，并且只是安排与销售人员的会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this email-based SDR and this human qualifying leads, which is not good for the customer.
zh: 所以这个基于邮件的 SDR 和这个人类对线索进行资格认定，这对客户是不好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't feel good to be qualified, does it?
zh: 被资格认定感觉不好，不是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: They will be mostly extinct next year.
zh: 它们明年大部分将会灭绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm guessing with your ... Now the AE, the classic human doing the sales, most of the tools aren't there yet for the most part.
zh: 我猜考虑到你的……现在那个 AE，那个做销售的经典人类，大多数工具在大多数情况下还不存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think 70% of their jobs will be safe by the end of next year, but I think it will decline to 40 or 50.
zh: 我认为他们 70% 的工作将在明年年底之前是安全的，但我认为它会下降到 40 或 50。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think there's any reason what we're seeing in other categories, a great agent can't close a deal too.
zh: 我认为我们在其他类别中看到的没有任何理由，一个伟大的智能体也不能完成交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: If there's not a lot to negotiate on price and the agent knows the product better than a human, at least for folks like you and me, I mean, do you like to talk to a human in sales?
zh: 如果价格上没有太多需要谈判的，并且智能体比人类更了解产品，至少对于像你我这样的人来说，我的意思是，你喜欢在销售中与人交谈吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Sometimes.
zh: 有时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'd rather just chat with a really smart AI.
zh: 我宁愿只是和一个非常聪明的 AI 聊天。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's all in progress now, but the classic ... And the tough part, and a lot of folks ask this question, Lenny, they say, "Okay, Jason, I see that in your data.
zh: 所以这一切现在都在进行中，但是那个经典的……而且困难的部分，很多人问这个问题，Lenny，他们说，“好的，Jason，我在你的数据中看到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: How are we going to build the sales profession if there's no entry level jobs in SDRs and AEs?"
zh: 如果 SDR 和 AE 没有入门级的工作，我们要如何建立销售职业？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a Meta question across all of AI.
zh: 这是一个跨越所有 AI 的 Meta 问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're already seeing AI concentrate strength in sort of mid-tier folks, isn't it?
zh: 我们已经看到 AI 将力量集中在中层人士身上，不是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're already seeing lots of folks cut back on entry level hires, Shopify's and others aside, where they'd rather have the six or seven-year-old engineer that's a cursor machine rather than train some kid.
zh: 我们已经看到很多人削减入门级招聘，除了 Shopify 和其他公司，他们宁愿拥有那个六七年经验的、是 cursor 机器的工程师，也不愿训练某个孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just more efficient today.
zh: 今天这样只是更有效率。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure you see that across a lot of folks you talk to.
zh: 我确定你在你交谈的很多人身上都看到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to happen in sales too.
zh: 这在销售中也会发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the folks that know how to manage an agent, work with an agent, the folks that know their product for real, they're going to become more valuable and the rest are going to become less valuable.
zh: 所以那些知道如何管理智能体、与智能体一起工作的人，那些真正了解他们产品的人，将会变得更有价值，其余的人将会变得价值更低。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's interesting because I'm an investor in a bunch of startups, as are you, and I'm actually seeing a lot of asks for go-to market people, salespeople.
zh: 这很有趣，因为我投资了一堆初创公司，你也一样，而且我实际上看到了很多对市场进入人员、销售人员的需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think this is kind of a temporary because there's so much demand they're like, "Oh, we need people to help."
zh: 你认为这是一种暂时的现象，因为需求太大，他们会说，“哦，我们需要人帮忙。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then, this will start to become more AI over time or are they just looking for these really senior people that you're talking about?
zh: 然后，这随着时间的推移会变得更加 AI，还是他们只是在寻找你谈到的那些真正高级的人？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, listen, whether you're managing humans or orchestrating agents, you need leadership.
zh: 嗯，听着，无论你是管理人类还是编排智能体，你都需要领导力。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've yet to produce an autonomous CEO.
zh: 我们还没有生产出一个自主的 CEO。

speaker: Unknown
en: I know folks talk about how ... on Twitter that AI will replace the CEO, but I don't know that that's literal as much as figurative, right?
zh: 我知道人们在 Twitter 上谈论 AI 将取代 CEO，但我不认为那是字面上的，而更像是一种比喻，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're still going to need the C-suite.
zh: 所以我们仍然需要 C-suite。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're still going to need VPs.
zh: 我们仍然需要 VP。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's become so much work to manage a million leads, right?
zh: 我的意思是，管理一百万条线索已经成为如此多的工作，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: A half a million leads.
zh: 五十万条线索。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we need these leaders whether ... The question is, and I've seen your call out there and I saw your tweet on it.
zh: 所以我们需要这些领导者，无论……问题是，我看到你在那里的号召，也看到你关于它的推文。

speaker: Unknown
en: The question is, how many of the folks that had the current playbooks are the right folks for the future?
zh: 问题是，拥有当前剧本的人中有多少是未来合适的人选？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm thinking maybe 20% of the folks I talk to.
zh: 我想可能只有 20% 的我交谈过的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: 20 are still panicking about AI and I'll tell you how to be in the 20% if you want to know, but I think very few like the Janine from Vercel are going to make the jump, so we'll see.
zh: 20% 的人仍然对 AI 感到恐慌，如果你想知道，我会告诉你如何成为这 20%，但我觉得很少有像 Vercel 的 Janine 那样的人能够实现跨越，所以我们拭目以待。

speaker: Unknown
en: There will be huge organizations, right?
zh: 会有巨大的组织，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Like Denise just went from Slack and Salesforce after 14 years to be CRO of OpenAI.
zh: 就像 Denise 刚刚在 14 年后从 Slack 和 Salesforce 离职，成为了 OpenAI 的 CRO。

speaker: Unknown
en: She's working going to be pretty high level, right?
zh: 她的工作将会是相当高层级的，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: So she may not need to know how to implement the agents, but most of the folks your companies want to hire, I would just make sure that they really want to roll up their sleeves and do the job of 2026, 2027, right?
zh: 所以她可能不需要知道如何实施这些智能体，但你们公司想雇佣的大部分人，我只会确保他们真的想卷起袖子，去干 2026 年、2027 年的工作，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Just because they worked at Slack does not necessarily mean they have the skills at your startup.
zh: 仅仅因为他们在 Slack 工作过，并不意味着他们在你的初创公司拥有所需的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: You said that you had some tips for folks to actually be this 20%.
zh: 你说你有一些技巧让大家真正成为这 20%。

speaker: Unknown
en: If someone's listening to this like, "Oh man, my job is in trouble.
zh: 如果有人正在听这个节目，心里想，“噢天哪，我的工作有麻烦了。

speaker: Unknown
en: What should I focus on?"
zh: 我应该专注于什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to sound simple.
zh: 这听起来很简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: It will work and almost nobody is doing this.
zh: 它会奏效，而且几乎没有人正在这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Pick a tool, an agent, an agentic tool to solve one of your problems.
zh: 选一个工具，一个智能体，一个具有智能体能力的工具来解决你的一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't almost ... Just one that's the most painful or the one that's most acute.
zh: 这几乎不……只要选最痛苦的那个，或者最紧迫的那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could be support, it could be SDR, it could be inbound qualification.
zh: 它可以是支持，可以是 SDR，可以是入站资格审查。

speaker: Unknown
en: Pick one, pick a leading vendor.
zh: 选一个，选一个领先的供应商。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't care which one it is.
zh: 我不在乎是哪一个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can talk about how to pick a vendor, but pick a leading vendor that treats you well, that you like, and do it yourself.
zh: 我们可以谈谈如何挑选供应商，但选一个待你不错、你喜欢的领先供应商，然后自己动手做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Train the agent, ingest the data, do the iterations, understand how this damn thing works.
zh: 训练那个智能体，摄取数据，进行迭代，理解这该死的东西是如何工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: The folks that are lost today have never done it.
zh: 如今迷茫的人从来没有做过这些。

speaker: Unknown
en: We literally ... we've turned it into a consulting shop, Lenny.
zh: 我们真的……我们把它变成了一家咨询公司，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's kind of crazy.
zh: 这有点疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know what I think about it, but we literally just did a job.
zh: 我不知道我怎么想，但我们真的刚做完一项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Amelia, our chief AI officer and Mia, she drove it.
zh: Amelia，我们的首席 AI 官和 Mia，是她主导的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We did a call with a public B2B company worth well over 10 billion that you would think is an AI leader, okay?
zh: 我们与一家价值远超 100 亿美元的上市 B2B 公司通了电话，你会认为它是 AI 领导者，好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And we did a call with their team and they're like, "We want to figure out this AI SDR stuff."
zh: 我们与他们的团队通了电话，他们说，“我们想弄清楚这个 AI SDR 的东西。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We're like, "Great.
zh: 我们说，“太好了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We think we're just going to buy this tool and just give it to our SDRs to figure it out on their own."
zh: 我们打算就买这个工具，然后直接把它交给我们的 SDR，让他们自己去弄明白。”

speaker: Unknown
en: One, no chance.
zh: 第一，没门。

speaker: Unknown
en: Two, we asked them, "How much of this have you done yourself?
zh: 第二，我们问他们，“你们自己做了多少这件事？

speaker: Unknown
en: Have you done it yourself?"
zh: 你们自己做了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was just crickets on this call of 20 people.
zh: 在这个 20 人的电话中，当时就是一片寂静。

speaker: Unknown
en: No one had done it theirself.
zh: 没有人自己做过。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they thought they could take an untrained agent with no training and just magically give it to a bunch of young 20-year-old SDRs and this magically would sell on its own.
zh: 所以他们以为可以拿一个未经训练的智能体，没有任何训练，然后神奇地把它交给一群 20 岁出头的年轻 SDR，这就神奇地能自己销售了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't work that way.
zh: 它不是那样工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the way all these agents work is there's a lot of jargon, which is intimidating, ingestion, orchestration, training.
zh: 所以所有这些智能体的工作方式是有很多行话，这很吓人，摄取、编排、训练。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that hard, guys.
zh: 这没那么难，伙计们。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just different.
zh: 只是不同而已。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the same B2B stuff we've been doing for over a decade.
zh: 这是我们十多年来一直在做的同样的 B2B 东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You go to a website, you give it a URL of your website, you give it a URL of what your Wiki is, you give it a URL of your training docs.
zh: 你去一个网站，你给它一个你网站的 URL，你给它一个你 Wiki 的 URL，你给它一个你培训文档的 URL。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe you upload your prospectus, you upload a few documents, it ingests the data and ingesting means it uploads.
zh: 也许你上传你的招股说明书，你上传一些文档，它摄取数据，摄取意味着它上传。

speaker: Unknown
en: It means it processes the data and it does some other stuff that you don't really need to know, some ragging, some vectoring.
zh: 意味着它处理数据并做一些你真的不需要知道的其他事情，一些 ragging，一些 vectoring。

speaker: Unknown
en: It really doesn't matter.
zh: 这真的不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: You upload some stuff and it kind of knows it and isn't great at it.
zh: 你上传一些东西，它就有点知道它，并且并不擅长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then ideally, it will turn it into questions.
zh: 然后理想情况下，它会把它变成问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... great at it.
zh: ……擅长。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then ideally it will turn it into questions.
zh: 然后理想情况下，它会把它变成问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you answer these questions and the more you answer and train it, training is just answering questions and getting better and better.
zh: 而你回答这些问题，你回答和训练得越多，训练就是回答问题并变得越来越好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So first you upload a bunch of your stuff, then you spend hours training it, often with the help of a vendor, someone called the Forward Deployed Engineer, which is a scary term, it means someone's going to help you do this.
zh: 所以首先你上传你的一堆东西，然后你花几个小时训练它，通常在供应商的帮助下，一个被称为 Forward Deployed Engineer 的人，这是一个可怕的术语，这意味着有人会帮你做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: You upload your stuff, you try to get it right, and then you basically have to make sure it's right.
zh: 你上传你的东西，你试着把它做对，然后你必须基本上确保它是正确的。

speaker: Unknown
en: QA, testing.
zh: QA，测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: And every day when that AI SDR sends out emails and do practice emails, they will say some dumb things.
zh: 并且每天当那个 AI SDR 发送电子邮件并进行练习邮件时，他们会说一些愚蠢的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe it's hallucinations, it really doesn't matter what the technical term is, and you correct it.
zh: 也许是幻觉，技术术语是什么真的不重要，然后你纠正它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you do this for 30 days, then every day you spend an hour or two correcting those mistakes.
zh: 如果你这样做 30 天，那么每天你花一两个小时纠正那些错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the 30th day, it's going to be pretty good.
zh: 到第 30 天，它将会相当不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyone can do this that has been in B2B or SaaS.
zh: 任何做过 B2B 或 SaaS 的人都可以做到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anyone can do what I just described.
zh: 任何人都可以做到我刚才描述的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is not that different than other things we've done.
zh: 这与我们做过的其他事情并没有什么不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just sequenced differently.
zh: 只是顺序不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: But nobody does this.
zh: 但是没有人这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone's panicked.

speaker: Unknown
en: It would save so much time, and it would do it 24 hours, then it would just set up the meeting.
zh: 它能节省大量时间，并且它能 24 小时工作，然后它就直接安排会议。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the reason that was a great second one was because no one was willing to do that.
zh: 所以那是一个很棒的第二个选择的原因是，没有人愿意做那件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: No human was willing to pick up the phone and talk to these people.
zh: 没有人愿意拿起电话跟这些人说话。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it was such low-hanging fruit.
zh: 所以这是唾手可得的成果。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the key to the first two, and if you're going to pick an agent, is they offered to help the most.
zh: 但是前两个的关键，如果你要选一个智能体的话，是他们提供了最多的帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the end of the day, Lenny, these are all running on Claude 4.
zh: 归根结底，Lenny，这些都是在 Claude 4 上运行的。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all basically using a bunch of APIs mashed together.
zh: 它们基本上都是使用一堆 API 组合在一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not new to software, mashing a bunch of APIs under the hood.
zh: 这对软件来说并不新鲜，在底层把一堆 API 组合在一起。

speaker: Unknown
en: But deep down, I don't want to trigger anybody, many of these, the leaders in AI GTM, they're more similar than different.
zh: 但在深处，我不想触发任何人，其中许多，AI GTM 领域的领导者，它们更多的是相似而不是不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're more similar than different under the hood.
zh: 它们在底层更多的是相似而不是不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't mean the features are parity.
zh: 这并不意味着功能是同等的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So because you have to train them, because it takes a month, the world's best software with no help training you is not one 99% of people should buy.
zh: 所以因为你必须训练它们，因为它需要一个月，世界上没有帮助训练你的最好的软件，并不是 99% 的人应该买的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the old days, we would qualify the best software.
zh: 在过去，我们会筛选最好的软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: We would make a matrix and we would do their thing and we would compare features and do it.
zh: 我们会做一个矩阵，我们会做他们的东西，我们会比较功能并做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've got to do another column, which is your Forward Deployed Engineer or solution architect or your SE and talk to them and say, "Who is going to help me?"
zh: 你得增加另一列，也就是你的 Forward Deployed Engineer 或解决方案架构师或你的 SE，跟他们谈谈并说，“谁会来帮我？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And before you write a check, get on the phone with Lenny and see if Lenny's really going to do deployment.
zh: 在你签支票之前，给 Lenny 打个电话，看看 Lenny 是否真的会做部署。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if Lenny rocks and the other vendor is better and won't help you, don't do it.
zh: 如果 Lenny 很棒，而另一个供应商更好但不会帮你，那就别做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why we've had so much success is the first two we did, yeah, they were startups, so they worked harder, but Artisan and Qualified just did the work with us.
zh: 这就是我们要么成功的原因，我们做的头两个，是的，它们是初创公司，所以它们工作更努力，但 Artisan 和 Qualified 确实跟我们一起做了这项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're not stupid, but it was work.
zh: 我们并不傻，但这确实是一项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We needed help.
zh: 我们需要帮助。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's what I learned is, you have this partner, the FDE and the vendor, and a lot of them actually might not take your business if they don't think they can help you.
zh: 所以这就是我学到的，你有这个合作伙伴，FDE 和供应商，其中很多实际上可能会如果不认为能帮到你就不接你的生意。

speaker: Unknown
en: The best ones turn away a lot of business today, which is interesting.
zh: 最好的那些今天会拒掉很多生意，这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: An interesting learning from this for folks is a lot of folks say, and they would say it to you if you use these, Lenny, they say, "You have too much data.
zh: 这里面一个有趣的收获，很多人说，如果你用这些，他们也会对你说，Lenny，他们说，“你的数据太多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: SaaStr is not like us.
zh: SaaStr 不像我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're a startup, we're tiny.
zh: 我们是一家初创公司，我们很小。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have 400,000 people in your database.
zh: 你的数据库里有 400,000 人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny has 1.2,000,000.
zh: Lenny 有 120 万。

speaker: Unknown
en: I only have 300 customers or 200 customers."
zh: 我只有 300 个客户或 200 个客户。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What I've learned is that's wrong.
zh: 我学到的是那是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have 300 customers, how many folks have come to your website ever?
zh: 如果你有 300 个客户，有多少人访问过你的网站？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many leads do you have?
zh: 你有多少线索？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many folks in your database?
zh: 你的数据库里有多少人？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many folks have you tried to reach out before?
zh: 你之前尝试联系过多少人？

speaker: Unknown
en: More than a human's doing.
zh: 超过人力所能为的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of a sudden they have aha moment.
zh: 突然之间他们有了顿悟时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: "How many folks do you have in your HubSpot?
zh: “你的 HubSpot 里有多少人？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many folks do you have in your CRM?"
zh: 你的 CRM 里有多少人？”

speaker: Unknown
en: They look it up.
zh: 他们去查了一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Okay.
zh: “好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: How many folks do you have talking to them?
zh: 有多少人在与他们交谈？

speaker: Unknown
en: Zero."

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't need the scale of numbers that you and I have to make these agents work.
zh: 你并不需要你我拥有的这种数据规模来让这些智能体工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need a little bit of scale and you need a little bit of traffic, but not as much as you think.
zh: 你需要一点点规模，你需要一点点流量，但并不像你想象的那么多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So all the learnings, we have a lot of folks that honestly, they don't want to do the work.
zh: 所以所有的经验教训，我们有很多人说句实话，他们不想做这个工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're like, "Well, SaaStr has a lot of scale.
zh: 他们会说，“嘛，SaaStr 有很多规模。

speaker: Unknown
en: They have a lot of years."
zh: 他们有很多年头。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not true.
zh: 这不是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it turns out to also be true with the training.
zh: 结果证明在训练方面也是如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm sure you've seen it with Lenny Bot.
zh: 我相信你在 Lenny Bot 上已经看到了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I thought having 12 years of content made the difference.
zh: 我以为拥有 12 年的内容会带来不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nah.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's having a couple months of really good content and a long tail beyond that, but you don't need as deep training and as much as you think.
zh: 只要有几个月的优质内容和之后的长尾内容就够了，但你并不需要像你想象的那么深度的训练和那么多的训练量。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just need a bit to be really good.
zh: 你只需要一点点就能做得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyone that has any scale whatsoever, even a couple million of revenue and up, can benefit from these products.
zh: 所以任何拥有一定规模的人，哪怕是几百万美元的收入及以上，都可以从这些产品中受益。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we did horizontal general bot, got us to a certain place.
zh: 所以我们做了横向的通用机器人，让我们到了一个阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then we did SDR for outbound, then we did inbound, and then we did Agentforce really early with Salesforce.
zh: 然后我们为外展做了 SDR，然后我们做了入站，然后我们很早就和 Salesforce 一起做了 Agentforce。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we didn't know what to do with Agentforce at first, but we decided we would reactivate the folks that sales decide was not worth their time.
zh: 起初我们不知道拿 Agentforce 怎么办，但我们决定重新激活那些销售认为不值得花时间的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Folks that reached out to sales, and this is true at every startup.
zh: 那些联系了销售的人，这在每家初创公司都是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We even just talked about some of the AI leaders where a human says, "You know what?
zh: 我们甚至刚刚谈到了一些 AI 领导者，那里的人类会说，“你知道吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think this is enough commission.
zh: 我觉得这点佣金不够。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm kind of busy.
zh: 我有点忙。

speaker: Unknown
en: I got a $4,000,000 deal with Meta going."
zh: 我正在跟 Meta 谈一笔 4,000,000 美元的交易。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We just took Agentforce just on those and we trained it on very similar prompt.
zh: 我们只是把 Agentforce 用在这些人身上，并且用非常相似的提示词训练了它。

speaker: Unknown
en: It had 70% response rate.
zh: 它有 70% 的回复率。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are people that were dying to interact with us.
zh: 这些人是渴望与我们互动的。

speaker: Unknown
en: 70% is so good.
zh: 70% 是非常棒的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is something humans were not willing to... it wasn't worth their time.
zh: 这是人类不愿意去做的……这不值得他们的时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know this sounds critical and maybe I'm going to trigger some sales folks, but the reality is if you're in a lead-rich environment, and I think that there's lead-rich and lead-poor environments for even big companies but startups.
zh: 我知道这听起来很挑剔，也许我会得罪一些销售人员，但现实是如果你处在一个线索丰富的环境，我认为即使是对于大公司，对于初创公司来说，都有线索丰富和线索贫乏的环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not enough, but eventually you become lead-rich.
zh: 线索不够，但最终你会变得线索丰富。

speaker: Unknown
en: Reps just don't follow up with a lot of them.
zh: 销售代表就是不会跟进他们中的很多人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just human nature.
zh: 这就是人性。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even you.
zh: 甚至是你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet more folks want to sponsor the newsletter than you can let in, right?
zh: 我打赌想赞助通讯的人比你能放进来的人多，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you pick up the phone with all of them?
zh: 你会接听所有他们的电话吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I replied to all of them and then we just tell them we're full, but yeah.
zh: 我回复了他们所有人，然后我们就告诉他们名额满了，不过是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you see the point, right?
zh: 但你明白这个意思，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Even your scale, you see the point, right?
zh: 即使是你的规模，你也明白这个意思，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it gets challenging.
zh: 是的，这变得具有挑战性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's imagine all of a sudden you had six months of inventory available.
zh: 让我们想象一下,突然间你有六个月的库存可用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet if you spooled up an agent and emailed all those folks back automatically, you'd fill up the docket, right?
zh: 我敢打赌,如果你启动一个智能体并自动给所有那些人回邮件,你会填满日程表,对吧?

speaker: Unknown
en: Mm-hmm.
zh: 嗯。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyone can do these sorts of... you think you can't, unless you're so small that you have sufficient humans to talk with every potential lead, every person that touches your website, every person that clicks with anything, you can benefit from AI.
zh: 所以任何人都可以做这类……你以为你做不到,除非你小到有足够的人类去与每一个潜在线索交谈,每一个触碰你网站的人,每一个点击任何东西的人,你都能从 AI 中受益。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was kind of our journey.
zh: 所以这就是我们的旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we've done a lot of other niche stuff.
zh: 然后我们做了很多其他利基的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll tell you at the end where we are today, maybe this is almost too much learning, is we're at the point where maybe we can't do one more.
zh: 我会在最后告诉你我们今天在哪,也许这几乎是太多的学习,就是我们到了那个点,也许我们不能做更多的了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because right now, when we did Delphi in the beginning, when I copied you with Delphi, even me, I spent almost an hour a day training it in the beginning because when we started to use it for support, it had an initial... it started telling people the wrong dates, and we could talk about why.
zh: 因为现在,当我们开始做 Delphi 时,当我用 Delphi 抄送你时,甚至是我,我起初每天花几乎一个小时训练它,因为当我们开始用它做支持时,它有一个初始……它开始告诉人们错误的日期,我们可以谈谈为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I had to fix it and it made some mistakes.
zh: 所以我不得不修复它,它犯了一些错误。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so when people started to use it, I had to spend an hour each morning firing up Delphi, reviewing the issues and answering them.
zh: 所以当人们开始使用它时,我每天早上不得不花一个小时启动 Delphi,审查问题并回答它们。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have to do it anymore.
zh: 我再也不用那样做了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's well-trained.
zh: 它训练有素。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have so many agents going and so many emails that Amelia has to spend 10 to 15 hours a week reviewing the outputs.
zh: 我们有这么多智能体在运行,这么多邮件,Amelia 每周不得不花 10 到 15 小时审查输出。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's exhausting because agents work all night and they work weekends and they work on Christmas.
zh: 这很累人,因为智能体整晚工作,他们周末工作,他们在圣诞节工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a big issue.
zh: 这是一个大问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Being the orchestrator or the chief AI person is not a good job for lazy people because the agents never sleep.
zh: 做编排者或首席 AI 人员对于懒人来说不是一份好工作,因为智能体从不睡觉。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it is so much time now to manage these 20.
zh: 所以现在管理这 20 个要花这么多时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is just interesting.
zh: 这只是挺有趣的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know when we're going to do the 21st.
zh: 我不知道我们什么时候会做第 21 个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We may be full.
zh: 我们可能满了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for folks that are startups, this is a reason to go harder because everyone was in market this year and it's going to keep happening, but business process change remains an issue for business software.
zh: 对于那些创业公司的人来说,这是一个更努力的理由,因为今年每个人都在市场上,并且这会持续发生,但业务流程变更仍然是商业软件的一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Business process change at the end of the day, and so many founders get this wrong, and 99% of sales folks, they don't care about business process change.
zh: 业务流程变更归根结底,这么多创始人搞错了这一点,99% 的销售人员,他们不在乎业务流程变更。

speaker: Unknown
en: Salesforce, they just want to get their commission.
zh: Salesforce,他们只想拿到他们的佣金。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't really matter what you pay for an app for a customer as long as it's fair.
zh: 你为客户付多少应用费用真的不重要,只要是公平的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's all the work to change the way you do your business.
zh: 所有的工作都在于改变你做生意的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're at the point where we're overloaded.
zh: 所以我们到了那个点,我们过载了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so just be aware, if you're a startup or even Salesforce or HubSpot, maybe close those deals in the next 12 months, because the window may close where people say, "Listen, that's the coolest agent I've ever seen.
zh: 所以请注意,如果你是一个创业公司甚至是 Salesforce 或 HubSpot,也许在未来 12 个月内关闭这些交易,因为窗口可能会关闭,人们会说,“听着,那是我见过的最酷的智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm exhausted from the last five.
zh: 我对之前的五个感到筋疲力尽。

speaker: Unknown
en: I had to do five last year.
zh: 我去年不得不做五个。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just literally cannot bring one more app into my enterprise."
zh: 我真的不能再把一个应用引入我的企业了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's going to be a headwind that today everything seems like it has tailwinds.
zh: 所以这将是一个逆风,而今天一切似乎都有顺风。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything's on fire, but people are going to get exhausted for having so many agents.
zh: 一切都在火爆,但人们会因为拥有这么多智能体而感到筋疲力尽。

speaker: Unknown
en: Exhausted.
zh: 筋疲力尽。

speaker: Unknown
en: Man, okay.
zh: 伙计,好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much to learn from in what you just shared.
zh: 你刚才分享的内容中有很多值得学习的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something I definitely want to ask about, as people hear this, agents sending off emails, agents talking to your clients, I get a ton of emails that are terrible.
zh: 我绝对想问的一件事是,当人们听到这个,智能体发送邮件,智能体和你的客户交谈,我会收到很多糟糕的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What have you learned about making these outbound emails good and not just noise?
zh: 关于让这些外发邮件变好而不仅仅是噪音,你学到了什么?

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you make these conversations high quality?
zh: 你如何让这些对话高质量?

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a really, really, really good question.
zh: 这真的、真的、真的一个好问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe the two biggest learnings.
zh: 也许两个最大的心得。

speaker: Unknown
en: Take your best person on your sales team, your best marketer you have, take their email copy and use that as the template for your AI.
zh: 拿你销售团队里最好的人,你拥有的最好的营销人员,拿他们的邮件文案并把它用作你的 AI 的模板。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone in 2024 said these products didn't work.
zh: 2024 年的每个人都说这些产品不起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: There were two reasons they didn't work.
zh: 它们不起作用有两个原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was before Claude 4.
zh: 那是在 Claude 4 之前。

speaker: Unknown
en: Replit didn't work.
zh: Replit 不起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lovable didn't exist.
zh: Lovable 不存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: Gamma didn't really work before 2025, right before Claude.
zh: Gamma 在 2025 年之前,就在 Claude 之前,并没有真正起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: The LMs reached this point where they would work for these use cases.
zh: LMs 到达了那个点,它们可以适用于这些用例。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was one threshold.
zh: 所以那是一个门槛。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other thing that happened in 2024 is the vendors kind of lied and said, "Just turn the product on.
zh: 2024 年发生的另一件事是供应商们有点撒谎，并说，“只要把这个产品打开。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll get you revenue.
zh: 它会为你带来收入。

speaker: Unknown
en: No need to train it, no need to do anything.
zh: 不需要训练它，不需要做任何事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll just do everything as this magic AI savant."
zh: 我们会作为这个魔法 AI 博学者来做一切。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not the way it works this way.
zh: 它并不是这样运作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What you do is an agent will be successful in go-to-market and sales today if you take what works for your best person, train... I know this seems like a scary term, but it's not.
zh: 你要做的是，如果你把对你最优秀的员工行之有效的方法拿来，训练……我知道这听起来像是个可怕的词，但它并非如此。那么一个智能体就能在进入市场和销售方面取得成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: Upload that text.
zh: 上传那段文本。

speaker: Unknown
en: Train the agent on it and let it iterate an A/B test from that.
zh: 基于它训练智能体，并让它从中迭代 A/B 测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Agents are really good at A/B testing.
zh: 智能体非常擅长 A/B 测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're really good at creating variants.
zh: 它们非常擅长创建变体。

speaker: Unknown
en: Ask Claude for a variant of your best email.
zh: 让 Claude 给你最好的邮件生成一个变体。

speaker: Unknown
en: Say, "Give me three versions of my best email."
zh: 说，“给我三个版本的我的最佳邮件。”

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll be pretty good.
zh: 它们会相当不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's all the agent has to do is take your best email you ever sent and stick it through an API.
zh: 智能体要做的就是把你发过的最好的邮件拿来并通过一个 API 发送出去。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm making it sound simpler than it is, but not by too much.
zh: 我说得比实际简单了点，但也没差太多。

speaker: Unknown
en: So train it and then what it'll do and then give it some data sources.
zh: 所以训练它，然后它会做什么，然后给它一些数据源。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the data source could be as simple as Salesforce.
zh: 数据源可以像 Salesforce 这样简单。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then if it has any data on Lenny, it can pull data and it can lightly personalize that email.
zh: 然后如果它有任何关于 Lenny 的数据，它就可以拉取数据，并且它可以轻度地个性化那封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even better, a lot of these products track all the visitors to your website.
zh: 更好的是，许多这样的产品会跟踪你网站的所有访问者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they can see what's happened and they use other APIs and so they can personalize your emails more.
zh: 所以它们可以看到发生了什么，它们使用其他的 API，所以它们可以更多地个性化你的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what ends up happening is the emails that the AI's write are pretty good.
zh: 所以最终发生的情况是，AI 写的邮件是相当不错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're getting terrible emails, it's a poorly trained product from a bad vendor.
zh: 如果你收到了糟糕的邮件，那是来自糟糕供应商的糟糕训练产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: You should be getting emails when you get them and you're like, "This isn't as good as Jason said on Lenny's podcast, but it's pretty good."
zh: 当你收到邮件时，你应该会收到这样的邮件，你会想，“这没有 Jason 在 Lenny 的播客上说的那么好，但也相当不错。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what AI can do today.
zh: 这就是 AI 今天能做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the magic is if a human isn't even doing that or if your mediocre humans are worse.
zh: 神奇之处在于，如果人类甚至没有做到这一点，或者如果你的平庸人类表现得更差。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll tell you, one of the first lessons I learned when my last startup was acquired by Adobe, Sam Blond was one of our sales leaders, then he became CRO of Brex and others.
zh: 我告诉你，当我的上一家初创公司被 Adobe 收购时，我学到的第一课就是，Sam Blond 是我们的销售领导者之一，后来他成为了 Brex 等公司的 CRO。

speaker: Unknown
en: We inherited a bunch of reps from Adobe.
zh: 我们从 Adobe 接手了一堆代表。

speaker: Unknown
en: We didn't ask for them.
zh: 我们并没有要求他们。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "My God, I never read everyone's emails before.
zh: 然后他说，“天哪，我以前从未读过每个人的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are the worst emails I've ever read."
zh: 这些是我读过的最糟糕的邮件。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So the AI can do better than that.
zh: 所以 AI 可以做得比那更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: The AI can do better than that.
zh: AI 可以做得比那更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you just train it on your best and it'll be pretty good.
zh: 所以你只要基于你最好的（表现）来训练它，它会相当不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just haven't seen a well-trained agent.
zh: 你只是没有见过一个训练有素的智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what I learned, and then another question folks ask is, "Okay, Jason, that email was pretty good.
zh: 然后我了解到，然后人们问的另一个问题是，“好的，Jason，那封邮件相当不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't as great as you said on stage, but it was pretty good."
zh: 它并不像你在台上说的那么好，但也相当不错。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But do you tell people it's an AI or do you hide it?
zh: 但是你告诉人们它是 AI 呢，还是你把它隐藏起来？

speaker: Unknown
en: And what we learned from sending hundreds of thousands is it doesn't matter.
zh: 我们从发送几十万封邮件中学到的是，这没关系。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're in an age where people don't really care as long as the email adds value and they know they're going to get an instant response.
zh: 我们处于一个人们并不真正在乎的时代，只要邮件提供了价值，并且他们知道他们会得到即时回复。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've tried both.
zh: 两者我们都试过。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've tried to say, "Hey, it's Digital Amelia or Digital Jason."
zh: 我们试过说，“嘿，它是 Digital Amelia 或 Digital Jason。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We've tried to fake it.
zh: 我们试过伪造它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what we've learned is now we just send it.
zh: 我们学到的是，现在我们只是发送它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We just send it and no one cares.
zh: 我们只是发送它，没人在乎。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially founders will get an email back, they'll be like, "Haha, I can tell this is an AI, but it's pretty good.
zh: 特别是创始人会收到一封回复，他们会像这样，“哈哈，我能看出这是 AI，但它相当不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can I do a meeting?"
zh: 我能安排个会议吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That kind of says it all, doesn't it?
zh: 那种情况说明了一切，不是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Mm-hmm.
zh: Mm-hmm.

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're creating issues as excuses to not do the work.
zh: 所以我们在制造问题作为不去做这项工作的借口。

speaker: Unknown
en: Your point about how human salespeople's emails are not great already is really powerful because all we're looking at is these okay emails from AI and you're saying, "Okay, but humans, they're not actually that much better if you actually look at them."
zh: 你关于人类销售人员本来就不擅长写邮件的观点真的很有力，因为我们看到的只是来自 AI 的这些“尚可”的邮件，而你却说，“但是，实际上看的话，人类也并没有好多少。”

speaker: Unknown
en: My God, they're not.
zh: 天哪，他们不是。

speaker: Unknown
en: Listen, the best outbound emails you've ever gotten.
zh: 听着，你收到过的最好的外拓邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: For example, I know you've done a bunch of investments.
zh: 例如，我知道你做过一堆投资。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of them are inbound to you.
zh: 其中很多是发给你的入站咨询。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want Lenny involved, right?
zh: 他们希望 Lenny 参与，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of them are just so good you can't believe it, right?
zh: 其中有些简直太好了，让你难以置信，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: A few.
zh: 有几封。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But a lot of them aren't, right?
zh: 但很多不是这样，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Right.
zh: 对。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mm-hmm.
zh: 嗯。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the best founders and the best sales execs and the best SDRs will spend two hours researching an email.
zh: 所以最好的创始人和最好的销售高管以及最好的 SDR 会花两个小时研究一封邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Okay, who exactly at IBM should I reach out to?
zh: “好吧，我在 IBM 到底应该联系谁？

speaker: Unknown
en: Who else exactly is a competitor that's using them?
zh: 还有谁确切是正在使用他们的竞争对手？

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly what was the ROI?"
zh: 具体的 ROI 是多少？”

speaker: Unknown
en: They'll give you a perfect story.
zh: 他们会给你一个完美的话术。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you get the world's best story, here's your competitor, here's how they use it, here's exactly when they bought, here's the ROI, here's the case study, that's a great email.
zh: 如果你得到了世界上最好的话术，这是你的竞争对手，这是他们如何使用它，这是他们确切购买的时机，这是 ROI，这是案例研究，那就是一封很棒的邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: How many 21-year-old SDRs do that?
zh: 有多少 21 岁的 SDR 会那样做？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, they use an automation tool, whether it's Outreach or Gong or Salesloft or MixMax or an AI-based, but they do no work.
zh: 不，他们使用自动化工具，无论是 Outreach 还是 Gong 或者 Salesloft 或者 MixMax 或者基于 AI 的工具，但他们不做任何工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to be that great.
zh: 那样做不会那么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to be that great.
zh: 那样做不会那么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's why people get a little confused.
zh: 所以这就是为什么人们有点困惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: The bar for good enough for AI GTM, it's not as high as we think.
zh: 对于 AI GTM 来说「足够好」的门槛，并没有我们想象的那么高。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just a facsimile of your best person reproduced as best we can.
zh: 它只是你最好的人员的一个仿制品，尽我们所能地复制。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to beat your mid-pack person.
zh: 它会打败你中等水平的人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to beat the person that literally knows nothing about your product.
zh: 它会打败那些对你的产品一无所知的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this an opportunity for humans to continue to thrive, this layer of much better emails?
zh: 对于人类来说，这是一个继续繁荣的机会吗，这层质量高得多的邮件？

speaker: Unknown
en: This came up when we had Jen Abel on the podcast.
zh: 当我们邀请 Jen Abel 上播客时，这个问题就出现了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I asked her, "What tools do you use?
zh: 我问她，“你使用什么工具？

speaker: Unknown
en: What do you use?"
zh: 你用什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: She's like, "Nothing.
zh: 她说，“没有什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just write it out artisanally."
zh: 我只是手工把它写出来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it works really well because everyone's sending AI emails.
zh: 效果真的很好，因为每个人都在发 AI 邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this just where go-to-market salespeople still can exist, this much better email, or is that also going to go away?
zh: 这就是上市销售人员仍然可以存在的地方吗，这种好得多的邮件，还是说这也会消失？

speaker: Unknown
en: Look, if you have a high-performing human team hunting high dollar value logos, and this is classic stuff, Lenny and I and Jen are in a conference room, we put a whiteboard of the 50 best folks that we want to sponsor Lenny's podcast.
zh: 瞧，如果你有一支高性能的人类团队在猎取高价值的大客户，这是经典的做法，Lenny 和我和 Jen 在一个会议室里，我们在白板上写下我们想要赞助 Lenny 播客的 50 个最佳人选。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's only 50 and we write Notion and we write Linear and we write Replit.
zh: 只有 50 个人，我们写 Notion，我们写 Linear，我们写 Replit。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's only 50.
zh: 只有 50 个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're all trying to sell them these new sponsorships.
zh: 我们都在试图向他们推销这些新的赞助项目。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're half a million bucks for two years.
zh: 它们是两年 50 万美元。

speaker: Unknown
en: Take it or leave it.
zh: 要么接受要么拉倒。

speaker: Unknown
en: We divide them up and we say, "Lenny's best at this, Jason's best as Jen," and we each take 15 or 17.
zh: 我们把他们分一分，我们说，“Lenny 最擅长这个，Jason 最擅长这个，Jen 最擅长这个”，然后我们每人负责 15 或 17 个。

speaker: Unknown
en: Dude, no need for AI there, is there?
zh: 哥们，那里不需要 AI，是吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Agreed.
zh: 同意。

speaker: Unknown
en: No need for AI today.
zh: 今天不需要 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because ROI is really high.
zh: 因为 ROI 真的很高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, and we're great.
zh: 是的，而且我们很棒。

speaker: Unknown
en: The three of us, we're different.
zh: 我们三个，我们不一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: The three of us are really different.
zh: 我们三个真的不一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to crush and we don't need any...
zh: 肯定会大获全胜，我们不需要任何……

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe one of us will take our emails and run it through Claude real quick just to make it better.
zh: 也许我们中有一个人会拿出邮件并通过 Claude 快速运行一下，只是为了让它更好一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or what I do is I do it for more research.
zh: 或者我做的做法是，我把它更多地用于做研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: I write the world's best email and then I say, "Claude, how could I make this better?"
zh: 我写出世界上最好的邮件，然后我说，“Claude，我要如何才能让它更好？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Do a little research, it will still be better.
zh: 做一点研究，它仍然会更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's an AI boost Jen should be doing.
zh: 所以那是 Jen 应该做的一次 AI 提升。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love Jen, but she should at least be making it better.
zh: 我爱 Jen，但她至少应该让它变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But for our 45, 50 best ones, we don't need it.
zh: 但对于我们前 45、50 个最好的客户，我们不需要它。

speaker: Unknown
en: What if it's 5,000?
zh: 如果是 5000 个呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Her approach just doesn't work.
zh: 她的方法就行不通了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So yes, a lot of the stuff we're talking about lends itself to higher-volume sales, but as everyone gets bigger, it's all higher volume.
zh: 所以是的，我们谈论的很多东西确实适用于更高销量的销售，但随着每个人变得越来越大，这都是高销量。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's just so much volume as you scale.
zh: 当你扩展时，量实在太大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, if you're tiny and you have three prospects and you're just getting into Y Combinator, maybe you don't need these tools, but we graduate out of that more quickly.
zh: 是的，如果你很微小，你只有三个潜在客户，而且你刚进入 Y Combinator，也许你不需要这些工具，但我们很快就会从那个阶段毕业。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the bespoke thing for Jen, I think, will work for high dollar value enterprise, but outside of that, I don't know, man.
zh: 对于 Jen 来说，这种定制化的做法我认为适用于高价值的企业客户，但在那之外，我不知道，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just can't touch enough people.
zh: 你就是无法接触到足够多的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Humans can't touch enough people.
zh: 人类无法接触到足够多的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And humans don't want to do the work.
zh: 而且人类不想做这项工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't want to talk to the mediocre leads.
zh: 他们不想和那些平庸的线索交谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll tell you an example.
zh: 我给你举一个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I was in London, I wanted to buy a $10,000 product and I'm literally in London and we're doing SaaS one.
zh: 当我在伦敦的时候，我想买一个 10000 美元的产品，而且我真的在伦敦，我们在做 SaaS。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have any time.
zh: 我没有任何时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: I get confused with the time zones, Lenny.
zh: 我对时区感到困惑，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if you do.
zh: 我不知道你会不会。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't even know what time it is in the Bay Area.
zh: 我甚至不知道湾区现在是几点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I just emailed this rep, it's the end of the year.
zh: 所以我只是给这个销售发了邮件，那是年底。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Just send me the contract.
zh: 我就像，“直接把合同发给我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to buy it."
zh: 我想买它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I have two questions.
zh: 但我有两个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have two questions I want answered.
zh: 我有两个问题想要得到解答。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I told them these two questions and they weren't even about price.
zh: 我告诉了他们这两个问题，它们甚至不是关于价格的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Took him three days to get back to me and he introduced me to someone else on his team.
zh: 他花了三天时间才回复我，并且把我介绍给了他团队里的其他人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wasn't worth his time.
zh: 这不值得他花时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: 10 grand wasn't enough because there's not enough commission for him.
zh: 10000 美元不够，因为对他来说没有足够的佣金。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he introduced someone else to me and the other guy said, "I can't answer your questions unless you'll get on the phone."
zh: 所以他把另一个人介绍给了我，而另一个人说，“除非你愿意上电话，否则我无法回答你的问题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I said, "I'm in London.
zh: 我就说，“我在伦敦。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm traveling."
zh: 我在旅行。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Ordinarily, I would've ended this, but it's a journey.
zh: 通常情况下，我早就结束这一切了，但这这是一段旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "If you answer my two questions, I will buy your product for 10K."
zh: 我就像，“如果你回答我的两个问题，我就会花 10000 美元买你的产品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "I need to get on the phone."
zh: 他就像，“我需要上电话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: AI's better than that.
zh: AI 比那更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is not Jen's and the whiteboard's thing of doing it.
zh: 这不是 Jen 和白板的那种做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even if Jen's process is right, AI can fill all the gaps.
zh: 即使 Jen 的流程是对的，AI 也能填补所有的空白。

speaker: Unknown
en: What about all the sponsors, the leads we didn't follow up with that we got a 70% response rate?
zh: 那所有的赞助商呢，那些我们没有跟进的线索，而我们获得了 70% 的回复率？

speaker: Unknown
en: The Jens are a diamond in the rough, whatever the expression is.
zh: 像 Jen 这样的人是沙里淘金，不管那个成语是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not that many of them.
zh: 没有那么多像他们那样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's not that many of them.
zh: 没有那么多像他们那样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love her and I love what she says and I agree with 99% of it, but here's a related point.
zh: 我爱她，我也爱她所说的话，我也同意其中的 99%，但这里有一个相关的观点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most of us don't have the hottest brands and we don't have the most elite CROs running them, so we end up settling for not the best sales team.
zh: 我们大多数人没有最热门的品牌，我们也没有最精英的 CRO 来运营它们，所以我们最终只能将就，用不上最好的销售团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the truth.
zh: 这就是真相。

speaker: Unknown
en: 99% of the best sales reps want to work just with the hottest brands.
zh: 99% 的最好的销售代表只想为最热门的品牌工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the minute your star fades just a little bit, they immediately want to jump to the next one.
zh: 而一旦你的星光稍微褪色一点，他们立刻就想跳到下一个热门品牌。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of reasons why.
zh: 原因有很多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Bear in mind, 99% of the world cannot attract a team of Jens for better.
zh: 请记住，世界上 99% 的人无法组建一个像 Jens 这样的团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just practical.
zh: 这只是务实的做法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even I can't.
zh: 即便是我也不能。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lenny, you're so great, but even a lot of folks that would want to go work for you if you wanted to hire someone, they'd be like, "Well, I love the... But what do I have to do?
zh: Lenny，你很棒，但即使是很多愿意为你工作的人，如果你想雇佣他们，他们会说，“好吧，我喜欢的……但我必须得做什么呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got to sell newsletter spon... No, no, no, no, No, no, no, no.
zh: 我得推销时事通讯赞助……不，不，不，不，不，不，不，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to be CRO at Lovable.
zh: 我想在 Lovable 当 CRO。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love Lenny, but is that really going to get me there?"
zh: 我喜欢 Lenny，但这真的能让我达成目标吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI can beat those.
zh: AI 可以战胜那些。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI can beat those.
zh: AI 可以战胜那些。

speaker: Unknown
en: But AI can't beat the enterprise thing.
zh: 但 AI 无法战胜企业级销售这回事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have no idea how AI is going to do in person sales.
zh: 我不知道 AI 要怎么做面对面销售。

speaker: Unknown
en: Someone smarter than me is going to have to answer that.
zh: 比我更聪明的人得来回答这个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have such a huge audience, Lenny, but I still think most of your folks are in tech and doing tech sales.
zh: Lenny，你的受众群体很庞大，但我仍然觉得你的大多数受众都在科技领域，做的是科技销售。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tech is the largest segment of our economy and growing.
zh: 科技是我们经济中最大的板块，而且还在增长。

speaker: Unknown

zh: 它们都很棒。
