en: They're very similar.
zh: 它们非常相似。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what it does is every single thing a human does is automatically tracked in your CRM instantly, real time.
zh: 它的作用是，一个人做的每一件事都会自动、即时地实时跟踪到你的 CRM 中。

speaker: Unknown
en: There have been tools that have done some of this before, but literally everything.
zh: 以前有一些工具做了一部分，但字面上是所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when we rolled it out and it happened with a few other folks I know, one of the folks on that old team quit that day.
zh: 当我们推出它时——我认识的几个其他人也发生了这种情况——那个老团队里的一个人当天就辞职了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Quit the day we rolled out the AI RevOps.
zh: 就在我们推出 AI RevOps 的那天辞职了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know why?
zh: 你知道为什么吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Hadn't done anything in 30 days.
zh: 他三十天什么都没做。

speaker: Unknown
en: The gig was up.
zh: 把戏被揭穿了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, wow.
zh: 哦，哇。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every day he would show up to our standup and he said, "Yeah, I'm doing outbound and I'm really working on that deal with Vercel and nothing would close."
zh: 每天他都会出现在我们的站会上，他说，“是啊，我在做外拓，我真的很在攻克 Vercel 的那个单子”，但什么也没成交。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we said, "Oh," and he quit that day.
zh: 然后我们说，“噢”，他那天就辞职了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So my point is he didn't lean into it, right?
zh: 所以我的观点是他没有拥抱它，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Lean into it.
zh: 拥抱它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you're going to have total transparency on your day.
zh: 现在你对你的一举一动将拥有完全的透明度。

speaker: Unknown
en: Granola is a side example for everything, but AI is going to track everything you do.
zh: Granola 只是一个万事万物的侧面例子，但 AI 将跟踪你所做的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you want to fight it, if you want to fight the future, good luck to you, but embrace it and you will leap ahead of your peers that aren't embracing this.
zh: 如果你想抗拒，如果你想对抗未来，祝你好运，但拥抱它，你将跃升到那些不拥抱这些东西的同龄人前面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Embrace all the transparency, all the leads, all the work it makes you do, because you can't close 10 times as much doing the same amount of work, can you?
zh: 拥抱所有的透明度，所有的线索，它让你做的所有工作，因为你不可能在做同样多工作的情况下成交十倍的单子，你能吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Nope.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: We do more work with AI.
zh: 我们用 AI 做更多工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm working the hardest I've ever worked.
zh: 我在比我以往任何时候都努力地工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: With all these agents and all the output they create, and Amelia does a lot of it, but even I am working the hardest, but it's better, but it's not less work.
zh: 有所有这些智能体和它们产生的所有输出，Amelia 做了很多，但即使我也在努力工作，但这是更好的，但这不是更少的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more work.
zh: 这是更多的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: The agents are so productive, you have to keep up.
zh: 智能体是如此高产，你得跟上。

speaker: Unknown
en: So sorry to interrupt.
zh: 所以抱歉打断一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: For the managers, buy an agent, deploy it yourself.
zh: 对于管理者来说，买个智能体，你自己部署它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't have someone else do it.
zh: 不要让别人去做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do everything from training to ingestion to orchestration.
zh: 从训练到摄取再到编排，什么都做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those terms will be less scary to you.
zh: 那些术语对你来说就不那么可怕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: For the junior folks, be the guy, the gal, the person that loves these tools and that is the first person to embrace whatever it is.
zh: 对于初级员工来说，做那个热爱这些工具的人，那个第一个拥抱任何新事物的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't fight it.
zh: 不要抗拒。

speaker: Unknown
en: Awesome.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: For founders in startups, what I'm hearing is be that forward deployed engineer, have that person, that sales engineer that's sitting there helping companies.
zh: 对于初创公司的创始人来说，我听到的是做那个前线部署工程师，拥有那个人，那个坐在那里帮助公司的销售工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is basically an opportunity to compete with bigger players where you're actually there helping them set things up.
zh: 这基本上是一个与大玩家竞争的机会，实际上你在那里帮助他们设置东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: All the companies you mentioned, none of them are big companies.
zh: 你提到的所有公司，没有一家是大公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're all startups, which is really interesting.
zh: 它们都是初创公司，这真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is true.
zh: 这是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I will say it is working with Salesforce the way we are.
zh: 我想说的是，我们正在与 Salesforce 合作，就像我们现在这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the way I haven't worked with Salesforce since it was the first product I bought, I mean, 20 years ago, dude.
zh: 这是一种我从 20 年前第一次购买它作为产品以来，就没有再与 Salesforce 合作过的方式，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I bought Salesforce 20 years ago, I got everything that was hands-on.
zh: 当我 20 年前购买 Salesforce 时，我获得了一切都是亲自动手的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I bought two seats.
zh: 我买了两个席位。

speaker: Unknown
en: I remember back in the day talking to my rep, I'm like, "Don't talk to me.
zh: 我记得当年跟我的代表谈话，我是说，“别跟我说话。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have time.
zh: 你没时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: You work at Salesforce.
zh: 你在 Salesforce 工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm buying two seats."
zh: 我买的是两个席位。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, no, no.
zh: 就像是，“不，不，不。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what we do.
zh: 这就是我们要做的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm here for you.
zh: 我在这里是为了你。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to help you get your app on the app exchange.
zh: 我会帮你把你的应用放到应用交换上。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to do all of it."
zh: 我们会做所有这一切。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that went away.
zh: 后来这消失了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's back.
zh: 现在它回来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Mark's got 2,000 folks doing this at Salesforce now.
zh: 现在 Mark 在 Salesforce 有 2000 人正在做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we have a forward deployed engineer.
zh: 所以我们有一名 FDE。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not even sure it makes economic sense.
zh: 我甚至不确定这在经济上是否合理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And maybe in two years, someone like you or I would not get an FDE, but the big companies are figuring it out.
zh: 也许两年后，像你我这样的人不会得到 FDE，但大公司正在搞明白这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yes, pick a vendor that will make you a success with an FDE.
zh: 但是是的，选择一个供应商，他会派 FDE 来让你成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people throw this term out, but again, it's just someone that's going to make sure you get trained and onboarded with your product for real.
zh: 人们抛出这个术语，但同样，这只是一个人会确保你真正接受产品培训并完成入职。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, so startups have a motivation to do this in a way that maybe some of the incumbents really are still figuring out, right?
zh: 但是是的，初创公司有动力这样做，而一些现有企业可能还在摸索，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Which is a big opportunity for startups to actually do this.
zh: 这对于初创公司来说是一个巨大的机会，去真正做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this actually resonates very closely to what Jen suggested for startups, which is sell services initially, just like do work for them, solve their problem, not with software to get things started, to grow that into a massive contract with them that is software.
zh: 这实际上与 Jen 对初创公司的建议非常接近，也就是最初出售服务，就像为他们做工作，解决他们的问题，而不是用软件来启动，以此将其与他们的一份巨额软件合同一起发展壮大。

speaker: Unknown
en: You mean being landed, expand, start with a smaller deal and grow it?
zh: 你的意思是落地、扩展，从小交易开始并发展它？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, a person sitting there doing this work with them, essentially what you're describing.
zh: 不，是一个人坐在那里与他们一起做这项工作，本质上就是你所描述的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't just like, "We have software, go try using it."
zh: 不要只是像，“我们有软件，去试着用它。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like we will have someone on our team helping you figure this out and maybe there's not even software yet to do all these things, but we'll have them do it for you.
zh: 而是我们会有团队成员帮助你搞清楚这一点，也许甚至还没有软件来做所有这些事情，但我们会让他们为你做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the software will take on more and more of that work over time.
zh: 然后随着时间的推移，软件将接管越来越多的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that's always been great advice.
zh: 我认为这一直是个很好的建议。

speaker: Unknown
en: And startups have always been great at this.
zh: 初创公司一直很擅长这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the early days, the CEO does it is doing support and CEO's onboarding you.
zh: 在早期，CEO 做支持，CEO 在给你做入职。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what's different today is the agent will fail without training and onboarding this product.
zh: 我认为今天不同的是，如果不对这个产品进行培训和入职，智能体就会失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: It will fail.
zh: 它会失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: It will never work.
zh: 它永远不会工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it becomes an imperative if you want to win.
zh: 所以如果你想赢，这变得势在必行。

speaker: Unknown
en: This term's been bandied about too much, but you need a team of humans, we can call them forward deployed engineers that make 100% sure that when the agent is turned on, it's awesome.
zh: 这个术语被滥用了，但你需要一个人类团队，我们可以称之为 FDE，他们要 100% 确保当智能体被开启时，它是令人惊叹的。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's your job as a founder.
zh: 这是你作为创始人的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Make sure it's awesome.
zh: 确保它是令人惊叹的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Versus in the old days, even when I built one of the first e-signature services that was so easy to use, we'd have some customers take them two years to go live, big customers.
zh: 相比过去，即使当我构建第一个易于使用的电子签名服务时，我们也有一些大客户花了两年才上线。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's just not okay in the age of AI.
zh: 在 AI 时代，这是不行的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Right?
zh: 对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And these forward deployed engineers, is that just another name for sales engineer or is that a different sort of background?
zh: 这些 FDE，这只是销售工程师的另一个名字，还是那是不同的背景？

speaker: Unknown
en: It can be.
zh: 可以是。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think all this nomenclature is confusing, man.
zh: 我认为所有这些命名法都令人困惑，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: All this orchestration and ingestion.
zh: 所有这些编排和摄入。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Palantir is obviously the single most successful public B2B company at the moment.
zh: 我认为 Palantir 显然是目前最成功的单一上市 B2B 公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe Databricks and a few others will beat them in the next wave of IPOs, but their idea for forward deployed engineers is similar, but these are nine figure deals.
zh: 也许 Databricks 和其他一些公司会在下一波 IPO 中击败他们，但他们关于 FDE 的想法是相似的，但这些是九位数的交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most of us aren't doing $100 million deals.
zh: 我们大多数人都不做 1 亿美元的交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the idea that you'll have Garry Tan and an army of folks out in the office for six months getting the software to work, that sort of inspires the idea, but it's really, sure, it could be a sales engineer or solution architect.
zh: 所以说,你要让 Garry Tan 和一群人在办公室里待上六个月让软件运行起来,这种想法虽然有点启发意义,但确实,当然,这可能是一个销售工程师或解决方案架构师。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the difference is the SEs that a lot of us worked with in the days were resources.
zh: 但区别在于,我们很多人当年共事的那些 SE 都是资源。

speaker: Unknown
en: And often, there was also a classic resource of like eight sales reps to one SE.
zh: 通常,还有一种经典的资源配置,就像是八个销售代表对一个 SE。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the eight reps, there'd be a pod and one SE would be responsible and you'd kind of have to fight to get Jason or Lenny's time to help the eight reps.
zh: 所以那八个代表,会有一个小组,一个 SE 来负责,你还得去争抢 Jason 或 Lenny 的时间来帮助这八个代表。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is inverted.
zh: 这反过来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: The FDE is number one job is to make the customer a success.
zh: FDE 的首要工作就是让客户成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 明白吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: I was literally the other day doing a presentation in an AI leader that closed a $3 million deal.
zh: 我前几天真的是在一个 AI 领导那里做演示,那是一笔 300 万美元的交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: The FDE did it all himself.
zh: 那个 FDE 全都是他自己搞定的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sales wasn't even involved in the deal.
zh: 销售甚至都没参与那笔交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: They went on site, they got the deployment going, they tuned it everything.
zh: 他们去了现场,他们把部署搞起来了,他们把一切都调优了。

speaker: Unknown
en: All sales did was manage it through the procurement process.
zh: 销售所做的只是通过采购流程来管理它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay?
zh: 好吗?

speaker: Unknown
en: That's pretty different than a guy answering some questions for the humans.
zh: 这和一个帮人类回答问题的人很不一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it is a combination of customer success and SE or whatever, but it is really... I mean, frankly, it's just being a classic consultant that gets the product done on day one, on day one.
zh: 所以这是客户成功和 SE 或其他什么的结合,但它其实是……坦白说,这就是做一个典型的顾问,在第一天就把产品搞定,在第一天。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the difference is that when you go live, it works.
zh: 区别就在于当你上线时,它是能用的。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you go live, it works.
zh: 当你上线时,它是能用的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's a fancy name for a bunch of folks on your team that when it goes live, the AI agent actually works.
zh: 这只是个花哨的名字,指你团队里的一群人,当上线时,那个 AI 智能体确实能工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have 100% success rate instead of like the 5% rate of 2024.
zh: 所以你拥有 100% 的成功率,而不是像 2024 年那样的 5%。

speaker: Unknown
en: So those people, but they do need to be technical.
zh: 所以那些人,但他们确实需要懂技术。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if they need to be engineers.
zh: 我不知道他们是否非得是工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can really vary based on... I love it when they're like mediocre engineers that are in love with the product.
zh: 真的可以视情况而定……我很喜欢当他们是那种平庸的工程师,但非常热爱产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's my favorite type of FDE.
zh: 那是我最喜欢的一类 FDE。

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't really want to code much anymore, but they did code and they just love your product.
zh: 他们其实不想再写那么多代码了,但他们写过代码,而且他们就是爱你的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I don't think it can be someone with no product chops, but all different folks can work, but they've got to know the product cold.
zh: 但我不认为这可以是一个没有产品手艺的人,但不同类型的人都可以做,但他们必须对产品了如指掌。

speaker: Unknown
en: But yeah, startups, I mean, the term is thrown around.
zh: 是的,初创公司,我是说,这个词被到处乱用。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need four folks that will just make sure that on go live, the product works, your agentic product works.
zh: 你需要四个人,他们只负责确保在上线时,产品能运行,你的智能体产品能运行。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's what you need.
zh: 这就是你所需要的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what might be useful to close out this conversation is to go through what are the things that are changing, like maybe a handful of things that are changed that are now going to be different in the world of sales go-to-market, and what are a few things that are just going to stay the same?
zh: 我认为要结束这次对话,可能有用的是过一遍哪些事情正在改变,也许有少数几件事改变了,现在在销售进入市场的世界里将会有所不同,以及有哪些几件事将保持不变?

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, let's go through it.
zh: 好的,我们过一遍吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, support is the first one to have changed.
zh: 显然,支持是第一个发生改变的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's our obviously permanently changed with AI.
zh: 这显然是随着 AI 永久改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whatever vendor you look at, 50 to 80% of support is done by AI.
zh: 无论你看哪个供应商,50% 到 80% 的支持工作是由 AI 完成的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we don't always think about support as GTM, but it is.
zh: 我们不总是认为支持是 GTM,但它确实是。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the start of a customer journey.
zh: 这是客户旅程的开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's very important to the customer journey.
zh: 这对客户旅程非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you're skeptical, go look at... Support has changed permanently.
zh: 所以如果你持怀疑态度,去看看……支持已经永久改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that train's left the station.
zh: 所以那辆火车已经出站了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Really, as we record this, not much has changed in sales.
zh: 真的,正如我们录制这期节目时,销售方面还没有太大变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I do think the stuff we've talked about is the bleeding edge.
zh: 我是说,我确实认为我们谈到的这些是前沿的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do think the classic cadence-based SDR running campaigns through a tool, him or herself, will be mostly extinct with 12 months.
zh: 我确实认为那种经典的基于节奏的 SDR 自己通过工具运行活动,将在 12 个月内基本灭绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is no reason AI can't do a better job than that role.
zh: AI 没有理由不能比那个角色做得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: The classic qualifier, qualifying inbound leads, which is a crappy experience for customers, should be similarly extinct within mostly extinct in 12 months.
zh: 那个经典的筛选员,筛选入站线索,这对客户来说是很糟糕的体验,应该同样在 12 个月内基本灭绝。

speaker: Unknown
en: The rest, we're going to wait and see.
zh: 其余的,我们拭目以待。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what we do know for reps, for sales reps, everyone wants to be even more efficient in the next 12 months.
zh: 我认为我们对代表们,对销售代表所知道的,每个人都想在接下来的 12 个月内更加高效。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of reasons people want to be... Again, at the bottom end, it's cost, it's profitability pressures.
zh: 人们想更高效有很多原因……同样,在低端,是成本,是盈利能力压力。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the high end, it's cultural.
zh: 在高端,是文化。

speaker: Unknown
en: We just don't want 200 reps running around Vercel or Replit that don't know what the product does.
zh: 我们只是不想要 200 个代表在 Vercel 或 Replit 跑来跑去,却不知道产品是做什么的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just the company owner, three to five million per rep is a lot different than three to 500,000.
zh: 就公司所有者而言,每个代表 300 到 500 万与 3 万到 50 万是非常不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to adjust as a rep.
zh: 所以你必须作为一个 rep 来调整。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every AI leader can't hire enough reps like we talked about, but you're going to have to adjust to being geometrically, if not exponentially more productive with help from AI.
zh: 每个 AI 领导者都招不到足够的 rep，就像我们谈到的，但你将必须进行调整，在 AI 的帮助下变得几何级数，甚至指数级地更有生产力。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to embrace these tools for real.
zh: 所以你必须真正地拥抱这些工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a lot of folks, a lot of old school GTM leaders are like, "AI isn't going to hurt sales reps.
zh: 很多人，很多老派的 GTM 领导者会说，“AI 不会伤害销售 rep。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just going to give them superpowers."
zh: 它只会赋予他们超能力。”

speaker: Unknown
en: The best ones, yes.
zh: 最优秀的那些，是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The mediocre are just going to be more mediocre.
zh: 平庸的只会变得更平庸。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think the AE is what we will always have salespeople, but being a people person is not enough anymore.
zh: 所以我认为 AE，我们总是会有销售人员，但做一个有人格魅力的人已经不够了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know how you can tell a mediocre salesperson, Lenny?
zh: 你知道怎么分辨一个平庸的销售人员吗，Lenny？

speaker: Lenny
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You ask them what they're really good at.
zh: 你问他们他们真正擅长什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a people person, Lenny.
zh: 我是一个有人格魅力的人，Lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know how good I am, Lenny?
zh: 你知道我有多厉害吗，Lenny？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm on text with 10 of my best customers.
zh: 我正在和我最好的 10 个客户发短信。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm a people person.
zh: 我是一个有人格魅力的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are the toughest technical objections you have at your product?
zh: 你在产品上遇到的最难的技术异议是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What?
zh: 什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: They don't know, but they're a people person.
zh: 他们不知道，但他们是一个有人格魅力的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is like golf 3.0.
zh: 这就像高尔夫 3.0。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not enough.
zh: 这还不够。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's insufficient.
zh: 这是不足的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So people are becoming obsolete in sales.
zh: 所以人们在销售中正在变得过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: Field sales, no idea how AI is going to impact that if you're out in the field.
zh: 现场销售，我不知道 AI 将如何影响它，如果你在现场的话。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, the enterprise leaders are hiring more field salespeople than ever.
zh: 我的意思是，企业领导者正在招聘比以往任何时候都更多的现场销售人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Salesforce is hiring more than ever and knocking on doors still works, man.
zh: Salesforce 正在以前所未有的规模招聘，而且上门推销仍然有效，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: So don't know the answers there.
zh: 所以不知道那里的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the office worker and the work from home worker, AI is going to take as much of your job or make you as much better as it can and you got to embrace it.
zh: 但是办公室职员和在家工作的人，AI 将会接管你的工作，或者尽可能地让你变得更好，你必须拥抱它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's, I would say, from support to knocking on doors, we're going to go from 80% to 0% from AI.
zh: 所以，我想说，从客服支持到上门推销，我们将从 80% 的 AI 采用率变成 0% 的 AI 采用率。

speaker: Unknown
en: What about phone calls?
zh: 那电话呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a great question and we should have hit it.
zh: 这是一个很好的问题，我们应该早点讨论它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, there's plenty of robocall and regulatory issues around it.
zh: 显然，有很多关于机器人电话和监管问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Certainly a lot of startups are breaking the rules anyway.
zh: 当然，很多初创公司反正都在打破规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would say this, listen, there's phone calls and there's even SMS.
zh: 我想说的是，听着，有电话，甚至还有 SMS。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's limits to how much SMS you can automate.
zh: 你可以自动化的 SMS 数量是有限制的。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of old school businesses don't even check emails.
zh: 很多老派的企业甚至不查邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, you're working on the shop floor.
zh: 我的意思是，你在车间工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So those are unanswered questions.
zh: 所以这些都是未解决的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are breaking the rules.
zh: 人们正在打破规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: OpenAI still breaks the rules.
zh: OpenAI 仍然在打破规则。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now it's licensing Disney content.
zh: 现在它在授权迪士尼内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: It used to just borrow Disney content.
zh: 它以前只是借用迪士尼内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: We will see.
zh: 我们拭目以待。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I don't know the answers to that.
zh: 所以我不知道那个问题的答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think in Europe, it'll certainly be much lower than in the US, but startups are going to push the limits.
zh: 我认为在欧洲，它肯定会比美国低得多，但初创公司将会推动极限。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're going to push the limits on what we can do with AI calls, AI enhanced, whether a human's kind of on the line, but AI is doing all the work, maybe that's legal, right?
zh: 他们将推动我们在 AI 电话、AI 增强方面的极限，无论人类是否在某种程度上在线，但 AI 正在做所有工作，也许那是合法的，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Getting more consent for SMS than we typically get.
zh: 获得比我们通常得到的更多的 SMS 同意。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to think that the typical barriers to robocalling and SMS that startups aren't going to bend the rules in the age of AI, I'm dubious.
zh: 所以认为典型的机器人电话和 SMS 障碍，初创公司在 AI 时代不会弯曲规则，我是持怀疑态度的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's a good question.
zh: 但这是一个好问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's harder to do.
zh: 这更难做到。

speaker: Unknown
en: The one thing I will add is, and it's a good objection to all of this, and I'd love to get Jen's thoughts too.
zh: 我想补充的一件事是，这对所有这一切都是一个很好的反对意见，我也很想听听 Jen 的看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you talk to the startups you'd invest in, Lenny, fewer of them are good at outbound phone calls than you'd think.
zh: 但是如果你和你投资过的初创公司谈谈，Lenny，其中很少有比你想象的更擅长外拨电话的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had many of the heads of revenue at Rippling, speak at SaaStr, the old CRO worked on my team and others.
zh: 我们有很多 Rippling 的营收负责人在 SaaStr 上演讲，前 CRO 在我的团队工作，还有其他人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were late to develop cold calling because we never did it back in the day.
zh: 他们开发冷电话开发得很晚，因为我们当年从来没做过。

speaker: Unknown
en: The CRO was on my team.
zh: 那个 CRO 在我的团队工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we had to bring in someone that had worked with Sam Blond at Brex who had done it.
zh: 所以我们不得不引入一个在 Brex 和 Sam Blond 共事过的人，他做过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a real art to pick up the phone and close business.
zh: 拿起电话并谈成生意是一门真正的艺术。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a specialized skill.
zh: 这是一项专业技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so if that's your specialized skill and there's no way for AI to benefit, so be it.
zh: 所以如果那是你的专业技能，而且没有办法让 AI 受益，也就这样吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I don't think for most tech calls that that is as impactful as we pretend it is.
zh: 但对于大多数技术电话来说，我不认为它像我们假装的那样有影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think most of the startups you and I work with and most of the folks listen to this do not close the majority of their revenue with cold human cold calls.
zh: 我认为大多数你我共事的初创公司以及大多数听众，他们并没有通过人工冷电话完成他们的大部分营收。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is a craft that works, but man, you got to be good at it.
zh: 这是一种可行的手艺，但是伙计，你得擅长它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this line somewhere that if you can close on a text message, AI can close it.
zh: 是的，有一条界线，如果你能在短信上成交，AI 就能成交。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is.
zh: 是这样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm being facetious in the sense that people say that I think have weaker relationships with customers.
zh: 我是在开玩笑地说，那些认为我与客户关系薄弱的人是这么说的。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is why people have weaker relationships with their customers than they think.
zh: 这就是为什么人们与客户的关系比他们想象的要薄弱。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if it's so easy to close the deal on a text message, and we've done hundreds of thousands of these and folks don't mind if it's an AI, if it's a good AI, why won't it close it on the text message?
zh: 如果在短信上成交这么容易，而且我们已经做了几十万次，如果它是一个好的 AI，人们不介意它是 AI，为什么它不能在短信上成交呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: AI can be people too.
zh: AI 也可以是人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It really can't.
zh: 它真的不行。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you don't believe me, folks, go to Lennybot.
zh: 如果你不相信我，伙计们，去 Lennybot 看看。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the old lennybot.com?
zh: 是那个旧的 lennybot.com 吗？

speaker: Lenny
en: Lennybot.com.
zh: Lennybot.com。

speaker: Jason Lemkin
en: If you don't think AI can be people, people spend hours on Lennybot, don't they?
zh: 如果你不认为 AI 可以是人，人们在 Lennybot 上花几个小时，不是吗？

speaker: Lenny
en: Yeah. And the best part is you could talk to Lennybot with voice. There's a voice feature that sounds exactly like me. It's unbelievable.
zh: 是的。最棒的是你可以用语音和 Lennybot 交谈。有一个语音功能听起来和我一模一样。这太不可思议了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a meta reason to go use Lennybot, go in with a learn in mind, don't go and bias.
zh: 去使用 Lennybot 的一个元理由，带着学习的心态进去，不要带着偏见去。

speaker: Unknown
en: You will find that AI can be people people.
zh: 你会发现 AI 可以是人人。

speaker: Unknown
en: People spend hours.
zh: 人们会花几个小时。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who are our best therapists today as we record this?
zh: 在我们录制这个节目时，谁是我们最好的治疗师？

speaker: Unknown
en: ChatGPT is our best therapist on planet Earth.
zh: ChatGPT 是地球上我们最好的治疗师。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a people person.
zh: 它是一个有人缘的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it sounds silly, but if that is your best defense in sales that you're a people person, the sands are sinking beneath you right now.
zh: 我的意思是，这听起来很傻，但如果那是你在销售中最好的防御，说你是一个有人缘的人，脚下的沙子正在下沉。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not enough of a skill.
zh: 这还不够算一项技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: People person is great when people buy enterprise software that's going to take two years to roll out and they have no idea how it works and it's a hope and a prayer.
zh: 当人们购买企业软件需要两年才能推出，而且他们不知道它是如何工作的，这完全是一种希望和祈祷，这时候「有人缘的人」是很棒的。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you expect the agent to work during the pilot before the big check comes, people person is insufficient.
zh: 当你期望智能体在开大支票之前在试点期间工作时，有人缘的人是不够的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Terrific.
zh: 太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tell me the person that's going to launch my agent, train it and get into production for me before I even pay you.
zh: 告诉我那个人，他将启动我的智能体，训练它，并在我付钱给你之前为我投入生产。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the dream.
zh: 这是梦想。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe this is one of the biggest changes of all.
zh: 也许这是所有变化中最大的变化之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I talk with Marc Benioff, and Salesforce is the biggest, right?
zh: 当我和 Marc Benioff 交谈时，Salesforce 是最大的，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's 44 billion.
zh: 它是 440 亿。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the biggest ship to turn.
zh: 它是最大的船，难掉头。

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "The number one thing I envy in Palantir, one is their high deal sizes."
zh: 他说，「我羡慕 Palantir 的第一件事是它们的高交易规模。」

speaker: Unknown
en: He made that joke in all the media and all the press.
zh: 他在所有媒体和所有新闻中都开了这个玩笑。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the other thing is, he said, "I wish I could.
zh: 但另一件事是，他说，「我希望我能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can't today.
zh: 我今天做不到。

speaker: Unknown
en: I wish every Salesforce customer now could go live before they pay."
zh: 我希望现在每个 Salesforce 客户都能在付款前上线。」

speaker: Unknown
en: That is so different from how we've been trained in many ways to almost rip off the customer, to get them to buy the product, first try to avoid a pilot, then have the smallest pilot we can, then roll it out over years to different people.
zh: 这与我们在许多方面被训练的方式非常不同，这几乎是敲诈客户，让他们购买产品，首先试图避免试点，然后进行我们可以进行的最小的试点，然后在几年内推广给不同的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI has upped the bar in terms of what customers expect.
zh: AI 已经提高了客户期望的门槛。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why the best ones are blowing up because the ROI is so high.
zh: 这就是为什么最好的那些正在爆发，因为 ROI 是如此之高。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you've got to deliver the ROI before the document is e-signed today.
zh: 所以你必须在文档今天被电子签名之前交付 ROI。

speaker: Unknown
en: People that haven't fully embraced that are at public companies growing 8%.
zh: 那些没有完全拥抱这一点的人是在增长 8% 的上市公司里。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're still trying to play games.
zh: 他们还在试图玩游戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are some other things that you think are going to change that people may not be thinking about that's going to change the way we do sales in the next couple years?
zh: 你认为还有哪些其他事情会改变，是人们可能没有考虑到的，将在未来几年改变我们销售方式的事情？

speaker: Unknown
en: Net net, we're going to need more sales and go-to-market professionals than ever because the winners are growing so quickly that even if they're more efficient, they will need more human beings than ever.
zh: 总的来说，我们将需要比以往更多的销售和 go-to-market 专业人士，因为赢家增长得太快了，即使他们效率更高，他们也比以往更需要人类。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'd have to put together a spreadsheet to see the crossover point.
zh: 我们必须得把电子表格凑在一起看交叉点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Obviously, many folks are shrinking headcount.
zh: 显然，许多人正在缩减人力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Microsoft's already said they're past peak employee.
zh: 微软已经表示他们已过了员工峰值。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're never going to be bigger.
zh: 他们再也不会变得更大了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're seeing this all across the companies we work with.
zh: 在我们合作的所有公司中，我们都能看到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: They want to be as lean as they can, but AI is such a huge part of our economy already.
zh: 他们希望尽可能精简，但 AI 已经是我们经济中如此重要的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's such a force of nature.
zh: 而且它是一股如此强大的自然力量。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everyone eventually goes enterprise.
zh: 最终每个人都会走向企业市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone eventually has a sales team.
zh: 最终每个人都会有销售团队。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone eventually does it.
zh: 最终每个人都会这么做。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's happening faster.
zh: 这发生得更快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: ElevenLabs, 50% of their sales is through enterprise now.
zh: ElevenLabs，50% 的销售额现在来自企业市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I don't know everybody.
zh: 我的意思是，我不认识所有人。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that Vercel just added Janine means they're going more enterprise by definition.
zh: 事实上，Vercel 刚刚添加了 Janine，这意味着从定义上讲他们正在更走向企业市场。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think Replit just added a sales team a couple months ago for real, and now they've added another CRO.
zh: 我认为 Replit 就在几个月前真正地添加了一个销售团队，现在他们又添加了一位 CRO。

speaker: Unknown
en: So they got to a hundred and something million with no traditional sales team, but at a billion, it's going to be flooded with salespeople.
zh: 所以他们在没有传统销售团队的情况下做到了一亿多，但在十亿时，将会挤满销售人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you get great at this stuff, if you go buy an agent today, when you listen to this and deploy it yourself and do the hard work and train it and ingest it and iterate it every day and get ahead of it and then get two agents and then three and then four, you may become more valuable, have a better experience in ETM.
zh: 所以如果你擅长这些，如果你今天去买一个智能体，当你听这个并自己部署它并做艰苦的工作并训练它并摄取它并每天迭代它并领先于它，然后得到两个智能体，然后三个，然后四个，你可能会变得更有价值，在 ETM 中有更好的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I believe, I hope, actually be better paid.
zh: 而且我相信，我希望，实际上是获得更好的报酬。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've talked about that we should have $250,000 a year SDRs, but they'd be like at Vercel, they'd be managing 10 agents, not 10 people.
zh: 我谈过我们应该有年薪 25 万美元的 SDR，但他们就像在 Vercel 一样，他们会管理 10 个智能体，而不是 10 个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then they're worth 250 grand.
zh: 那么他们就值 25 万。

speaker: Unknown
en: For sell, they'd be managing 10 agents, not 10 people.
zh: 对于销售，他们将管理 10 个智能体，而不是 10 个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Then they're worth 250 grand, instead of 80 grand or 90 grand.
zh: 那么他们就值 25 万，而不是 8 万或 9 万。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that much, is it?
zh: 这不是很多，是吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: So, there is a great world coming.
zh: 所以，一个美好的世界即将到来。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are the best of times, aren't they, Lenny, for product and business?
zh: 这是最好的时代，对吧，Lenny，对于产品和业务来说？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not evenly distributed.
zh: 但这并不是均匀分布的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So, if you have those skills, even though we're not going to need these SDRs and even though not going to be BDRs, even though we can get rid of half of AEs, the amount of revenue and growth in AI leaders is so phenomenal, right?
zh: 所以，如果你拥有那些技能，尽管我们将不再需要这些 SDR，尽管不再需要 BDR，尽管我们可以裁掉一半的 AE，但 AI 领导者的收入和增长量是如此惊人，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: And not just at the startups, at the Google Clouds, at everywhere.
zh: 而且不仅仅是在初创公司，在 Google Clouds，在任何地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: Azure, they're hiring so many humans that net-net, it's a positive for the profession, but not for the way we've done it in the past.
zh: Azure，他们正在雇佣如此多的人类，总的来说，这对这个行业是积极的，但对于我们过去的那种方式则不然。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're at risk.
zh: 你正处于风险之中。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is really interesting that we're still hiring.
zh: 我们还在招聘，这真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is what I've seen too, just like everyone's hiring salespeople, go-to-market people.
zh: 这也是我所看到的，就像大家都在招聘销售人员、go-to-market 人员一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think there's going to be this peak in the next couple years of just, okay, now that AI is doing more and more of this, or is it just hard to predict because who knows how big AI gets, how big these companies get?
zh: 你认为在未来几年会出现这样一个峰值吗，就是好吧，既然 AI 正在做越来越多的事情，还是这很难预测，因为谁知道 AI 会变得多大，这些公司会变得多大？

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, IT's over a trillion dollars, and it's accelerating.
zh: 我的意思是，IT 已经超过一万亿美元，而且正在加速。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is increasing the amount.
zh: AI 正在增加这个数量。

speaker: Unknown
en: Gartner says next year will be the fastest acceleration of money deployed into IT and software in a decade.
zh: Gartner 说明年将是资金部署到 IT 和软件中最快加速的一年。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's reaccelerated.
zh: 它重新加速了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can that last forever?
zh: 这能永远持续下去吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No.
zh: 不。

speaker: Unknown
en: Eventually we consume 100% of the global GDP.
zh: 最终我们将消耗全球 GDP 的 100%。
